860	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the un-localised section Čech module complex `D•`
861	
862	`D^m` is the product `∏_{σ : Fin m → ι} M_{s_σ}` of away-localisations of
863	`M = Γ(Spec R, F)` at the multi-index products `s_σ = ∏ₖ s_{σ k}`; its differential
864	is the alternating sum of the canonical localisation comparison maps (index deletion
865	drops a factor, so `s_{σ∘dⱼ} ∣ s_σ`).  This is the concrete `R`-module complex `D•`
866	of `lem:section_cech_homology_exact` that the section Čech complex is identified
867	with.  Positive-degree exactness is reduced — via `exact_of_isLocalized_span` — to
868	the localised complexes already shown exact in `CechLocalized.cechLocalized_exact`;
869	the bridge is the localisation-transitivity lemma
870	`AwayComparison.comparison_isLocalizedModule` (applied here as
871	`dToCech_isLocalizedModule`). -/
872	
873	namespace SectionCechModule
874	
875	open CechLocalized AwayComparison
876	
877	variable {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {ι : Type*} (s : ι → R)
878	variable (M : Type u) [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M]
879	
880	/-- The un-localised section Čech coefficient `D_σ = M_{s_σ}`. -/
881	abbrev dCoeff {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin m → ι) : Type u :=
882	  LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (sprod s σ)) M
883	
884	/-- The un-localised coface comparison `δ : M_{s_{σ∘dⱼ}} → M_{s_σ}` (R-linear), the
885	canonical localisation map for the divisibility `s_{σ∘dⱼ} ∣ s_σ`. -/
886	noncomputable def dCoface (m : ℕ) (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι) (j : Fin (m + 1)) :
887	    dCoeff s M (σ ∘ j.succAbove) →ₗ[R] dCoeff s M σ :=
888	  comparison
889	    (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (sprod s (σ ∘ j.succAbove))) M)
890	    (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (sprod s σ)) M)
891	    (Inverts.of_dvd (sprod_succAbove_dvd s σ j)
892	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (sprod s σ)) M))
893	
894	/-- The un-localised differential `d : D^m → D^{m+1}` as an `R`-linear map: the
895	alternating sum of cofaces, assembled with `LinearMap.pi` over the output tuple. -/
896	noncomputable def dDiff (m : ℕ) :
897	    (∀ σ : Fin m → ι, dCoeff s M σ) →ₗ[R] (∀ σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι, dCoeff s M σ) :=
898	  LinearMap.pi fun σ => ∑ j : Fin (m + 1),
899	    (-1 : ℤ) ^ (j : ℕ) • (dCoface s M m σ j ∘ₗ LinearMap.proj (σ ∘ j.succAbove))
900	
901	/-- Apply form of `dDiff`: the alternating sum of cofaces of the deleted faces. -/
902	lemma dDiff_apply (m : ℕ) (t : ∀ σ : Fin m → ι, dCoeff s M σ) (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι) :
903	    dDiff s M m t σ
904	      = ∑ j : Fin (m + 1), (-1 : ℤ) ^ (j : ℕ) • dCoface s M m σ j (t (σ ∘ j.succAbove)) := by
905	  rw [dDiff, LinearMap.pi_apply, LinearMap.sum_apply]
906	  exact Finset.sum_congr rfl fun d _ => rfl
907	
908	variable (r : ι)
909	
910	/-- The per-index localisation comparison `M_{s_σ} → M_{s_r · s_σ}` carrying the
911	un-localised coefficient to the `cechCoeff` coefficient localised at `s_r`. -/
912	noncomputable def dToCech {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin m → ι) :
913	    dCoeff s M σ →ₗ[R] cechCoeff s M r σ :=
914	  comparison
915	    (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (sprod s σ)) M)
916	    (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (s r * sprod s σ)) M)
917	    (Inverts.of_dvd (dvd_mul_left (sprod s σ) (s r))
918	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (s r * sprod s σ)) M))
919	
920	/-- **Transitivity payoff**: `dToCech` exhibits the localised coefficient
921	`cechCoeff = M_{s_r · s_σ}` as the localisation of the un-localised coefficient
922	`M_{s_σ}` away from the spanning element `s_r`.  Direct from
923	`AwayComparison.comparison_isLocalizedModule`; this is the per-coefficient input the
924	`exact_of_isLocalized_span` reduction of the section Čech complex consumes. -/
925	lemma dToCech_isLocalizedModule {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin m → ι) :
926	    IsLocalizedModule.Away (s r) (dToCech s M r σ) := by
927	  haveI inst : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (sprod s σ * s r))
928	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (s r * sprod s σ)) M) := by
929	    rw [mul_comm]; infer_instance
930	  unfold dToCech
931	  exact comparison_isLocalizedModule (a := sprod s σ) (b := s r) _ _ _
932	
933	/-- **Per-coface localisation naturality**: the localised coface `cechCoface`
934	intertwines the per-index comparison `dToCech` with the un-localised coface
935	`dCoface`.  Both composites are away-comparison maps from `M_{s_{σ∘dⱼ}}` to
936	`M_{s_r · s_σ}`, hence equal by `comparison_comp`.  This is the square that, summed
937	over the alternating signs, identifies the localised differential
938	`IsLocalizedModule.map (dDiff)` with `CombinatorialCech.depDiff (cechCoface)` —
939	the final brick of the `exact_of_isLocalized_span` reduction. -/
940	lemma cechCoface_dToCech {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι) (j : Fin (m + 1))
941	    (x : dCoeff s M (σ ∘ j.succAbove)) :
942	    cechCoface s M r m σ j (dToCech s M r (σ ∘ j.succAbove) x)
943	      = dToCech s M r σ (dCoface s M m σ j x) := by
944	  simp only [cechCoface, dToCech, dCoface, LinearMap.toAddMonoidHom_coe]
945	  rw [comparison_comp_apply, comparison_comp_apply]
946	  exact Inverts.of_dvd (dvd_trans (sprod_succAbove_dvd s σ j) (dvd_mul_left (sprod s σ) (s r)))
947	    (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (s r * sprod s σ)) M)
948	
949	/-- **Differential-naturality square** (the heart of the `exact_of_isLocalized_span`
950	reduction): localising the un-localised differential `dDiff` at a spanning element
951	`s_r` — via the per-index comparisons `dToCech` — yields the localised differential
952	`CombinatorialCech.depDiff (cechCoface)`.  Summing the per-coface squares
953	`cechCoface_dToCech` over the alternating signs.  Combined with
954	`dToCech_isLocalizedModule`, `IsLocalizedModule.pi`, and the uniqueness of
955	`IsLocalizedModule.map`, this identifies the localised `dDiff` with the
956	exact complex `CechLocalized.cechLocalized_exact`. -/
957	lemma dToCech_comm (m : ℕ) (t : ∀ σ : Fin m → ι, dCoeff s M σ) :
958	    CombinatorialCech.depDiff (A := fun _ σ => cechCoeff s M r σ) (cechCoface s M r) (m := m)
959	        (fun σ => dToCech s M r σ (t σ))
960	      = fun σ => dToCech s M r σ (dDiff s M m t σ) := by
961	  funext σ
962	  simp only [CombinatorialCech.depDiff, dDiff_apply, map_sum]
963	  refine Finset.sum_congr rfl fun j _ => ?_
964	  rw [map_zsmul, cechCoface_dToCech]
965	
966	/-! ### Assembly of step (a): positive-degree exactness of `D•`
967	
968	The localised differential at a fixed index `r`, bundled as an `R`-linear map
969	(`locDiff`), so that `IsLocalizedModule.map` uniqueness identifies it with the
970	localisation of `dDiff`.  Everything here is at a *fixed* index `r : ι` (no
971	`Classical.choose`), hence friction-free; the spanning-element bookkeeping is
972	confined to the final `dDiff_exact`. -/
973	
974	/-- R-linear underlying map of the localised coface `cechCoface` (the `comparison`
975	before `.toAddMonoidHom`). -/
976	noncomputable def cechCofaceLin (r : ι) (m : ℕ) (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι) (j : Fin (m + 1)) :
977	    cechCoeff s M r (σ ∘ j.succAbove) →ₗ[R] cechCoeff s M r σ :=
978	  comparison
979	    (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (s r * sprod s (σ ∘ j.succAbove))) M)
980	    (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (s r * sprod s σ)) M)
981	    (Inverts.of_dvd (mul_dvd_mul_left (s r) (sprod_succAbove_dvd s σ j))
982	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (s r * sprod s σ)) M))
983	
984	lemma cechCoface_apply (r : ι) (m : ℕ) (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι) (j : Fin (m + 1))
985	    (x : cechCoeff s M r (σ ∘ j.succAbove)) :
986	    cechCoface s M r m σ j x = cechCofaceLin s M r m σ j x := rfl
987	
988	/-- The localised section Čech differential at index `r`, as a bundled `R`-linear
989	map (mirrors `dDiff` with the localised cofaces). -/
990	noncomputable def locDiff (r : ι) (m : ℕ) :
991	    (∀ σ : Fin m → ι, cechCoeff s M r σ) →ₗ[R] (∀ σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι, cechCoeff s M r σ) :=
992	  LinearMap.pi fun σ => ∑ j : Fin (m + 1),
993	    (-1 : ℤ) ^ (j : ℕ) • (cechCofaceLin s M r m σ j ∘ₗ LinearMap.proj (σ ∘ j.succAbove))
994	
995	lemma locDiff_apply (r : ι) (m : ℕ) (t : ∀ σ : Fin m → ι, cechCoeff s M r σ)
996	    (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι) :
997	    locDiff s M r m t σ
998	      = ∑ j : Fin (m + 1),
999	          (-1 : ℤ) ^ (j : ℕ) • cechCofaceLin s M r m σ j (t (σ ∘ j.succAbove)) := by
1000	  rw [locDiff, LinearMap.pi_apply, LinearMap.sum_apply]
1001	  exact Finset.sum_congr rfl fun d _ => rfl
1002	
1003	/-- `locDiff` agrees, as a raw function, with the localised combinatorial
1004	differential `CombinatorialCech.depDiff (cechCoface)` already shown exact. -/
1005	lemma locDiff_eq_depDiff (r : ι) (m : ℕ) :
1006	    ⇑(locDiff s M r m)
1007	      = CombinatorialCech.depDiff (A := fun _ σ => cechCoeff s M r σ)
1008	          (cechCoface s M r) (m := m) := by
1009	  funext t σ
1010	  rw [locDiff_apply]
1011	  simp only [CombinatorialCech.depDiff]
1012	  exact Finset.sum_congr rfl fun j _ => by rw [cechCoface_apply]
1013	
1014	/-- Positive-degree exactness of the bundled localised differential, transported
1015	from `CechLocalized.cechLocalized_exact`. -/
1016	lemma locDiff_exact (r : ι) (m : ℕ) :
1017	    Function.Exact (locDiff s M r (m + 1)) (locDiff s M r (m + 2)) := by
1018	  rw [locDiff_eq_depDiff, locDiff_eq_depDiff]
1019	  exact CechLocalized.cechLocalized_exact s M r m
1020	
1021	/-- The product localisation map `D^m → ∏_σ M_{s_r·s_σ}` at index `r`. -/
1022	noncomputable def fLoc (r : ι) (m : ℕ) :
1023	    (∀ σ : Fin m → ι, dCoeff s M σ) →ₗ[R] (∀ σ : Fin m → ι, cechCoeff s M r σ) :=
1024	  LinearMap.pi fun σ => dToCech s M r σ ∘ₗ LinearMap.proj σ
1025	
1026	lemma fLoc_apply (r : ι) (m : ℕ) (t : ∀ σ : Fin m → ι, dCoeff s M σ) (σ : Fin m → ι) :
1027	    fLoc s M r m t σ = dToCech s M r σ (t σ) := by
1028	  simp only [fLoc, LinearMap.pi_apply, LinearMap.comp_apply, LinearMap.proj_apply]
1029	
1030	/-- `fLoc` exhibits the product localised coefficient as the localisation of `D^m`
1031	away from `s_r` (from `IsLocalizedModule.pi` + `dToCech_isLocalizedModule`). -/
1032	instance fLoc_isLocalizedModule [Finite ι] (r : ι) (m : ℕ) :
1033	    IsLocalizedModule.Away (s r) (fLoc s M r m) := by
1034	  haveI : ∀ σ : Fin m → ι, IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (s r)) (dToCech s M r σ) :=
1035	    fun σ => dToCech_isLocalizedModule s M r σ
1036	  exact IsLocalizedModule.pi (Submonoid.powers (s r)) fun σ => dToCech s M r σ
1037	
1038	/-- The localised differential intertwines `fLoc` with `dDiff` (the `dToCech_comm`
1039	square, packaged through the bundled maps). -/
1040	lemma locDiff_fLoc (r : ι) (m : ℕ) (t : ∀ σ : Fin m → ι, dCoeff s M σ) :
1041	    locDiff s M r m (fLoc s M r m t) = fLoc s M r (m + 1) (dDiff s M m t) := by
1042	  have h1 : ⇑(locDiff s M r m) (fLoc s M r m t)
1043	      = CombinatorialCech.depDiff (A := fun _ σ => cechCoeff s M r σ) (cechCoface s M r) (m := m)
1044	          (fun σ => dToCech s M r σ (t σ)) := by
1045	    rw [locDiff_eq_depDiff]
1046	    exact congrArg _ (funext fun σ => fLoc_apply s M r m t σ)
1047	  rw [h1, dToCech_comm]
1048	  funext σ
1049	  exact (fLoc_apply s M r (m + 1) (dDiff s M m t) σ).symm
1050	
1051	/-- `IsLocalizedModule.map` of `dDiff` is the bundled localised differential
1052	`locDiff` — by `IsLocalizedModule.ext`, the comparison reducing to `locDiff_fLoc`.
1053	Stated for an arbitrary away element `a` (with `s r = a`) so the spanning-element
1054	bookkeeping in `dDiff_exact` needs no `↑ρ`-rewrite inside the localised map. -/
1055	lemma map_dDiff_eq_locDiff (r : ι) (m : ℕ) {a : R}
1056	    [IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers a) (fLoc s M r m)]
1057	    [IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers a) (fLoc s M r (m + 1))] :
1058	    IsLocalizedModule.map (Submonoid.powers a) (fLoc s M r m) (fLoc s M r (m + 1))
1059	        (dDiff s M m) = locDiff s M r m := by
1060	  apply IsLocalizedModule.ext (Submonoid.powers a) (fLoc s M r m)
1061	    (fun x => IsLocalizedModule.map_units (fLoc s M r (m + 1)) x)
1062	  apply LinearMap.ext; intro t
1063	  rw [LinearMap.comp_apply, LinearMap.comp_apply, IsLocalizedModule.map_apply, locDiff_fLoc]
1064	
1065	/-- A chosen `ι`-index realising a spanning-set element as `s i`, kept opaque so
1066	the spanning-element rewrite in `dDiff_exact` has a type-correct motive (the index
1067	must not syntactically contain `↑ρ`). -/
1068	private noncomputable def spanIdx (ρ : ↑(Set.range s)) : ι := ρ.2.choose
1069	
1070	omit [CommRing R] in
1071	private lemma spanIdx_spec (ρ : ↑(Set.range s)) : s (spanIdx s ρ) = ↑ρ := ρ.2.choose_spec
1072	
1073	/-- **Step (a): positive-degree exactness of the un-localised section Čech module
1074	complex `D•`** (`lem:cech_acyclic_affine`, the `R`-module core).  For a spanning
1075	family `s : ι → R` (`Ideal.span (Set.range s) = ⊤`), the complex
1076	`∏_σ M_{s_σ}` is exact in positive degrees.  Reduced — via
1077	`exact_of_isLocalized_span` localising at each spanning element `s_r` — to the
1078	exactness `locDiff_exact` of the localised complexes (which is the dependent
1079	combinatorial core `CechLocalized.cechLocalized_exact`), through the localised-
1080	differential identification `map_dDiff_eq_locDiff`. -/
1081	lemma dDiff_exact [Finite ι] (hs : Ideal.span (Set.range s) = ⊤) (m : ℕ) :
1082	    Function.Exact (dDiff s M (m + 1)) (dDiff s M (m + 2)) := by
1083	  classical
1084	  haveI inst1 : ∀ ρ : ↑(Set.range s),
1085	      IsLocalizedModule.Away (↑ρ) (fLoc s M (spanIdx s ρ) (m + 1)) := fun ρ => by
1086	    rw [← spanIdx_spec s ρ]; infer_instance
1087	  haveI inst2 : ∀ ρ : ↑(Set.range s),
1088	      IsLocalizedModule.Away (↑ρ) (fLoc s M (spanIdx s ρ) (m + 2)) := fun ρ => by
1089	    rw [← spanIdx_spec s ρ]; infer_instance
1090	  haveI inst3 : ∀ ρ : ↑(Set.range s),
1091	      IsLocalizedModule.Away (↑ρ) (fLoc s M (spanIdx s ρ) (m + 3)) := fun ρ => by
1092	    rw [← spanIdx_spec s ρ]; infer_instance
1093	  refine exact_of_isLocalized_span (Set.range s) hs
1094	    (fun ρ => ∀ σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι, cechCoeff s M (spanIdx s ρ) σ)
1095	    (fun ρ => fLoc s M (spanIdx s ρ) (m + 1))
1096	    (fun ρ => ∀ σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι, cechCoeff s M (spanIdx s ρ) σ)
1097	    (fun ρ => fLoc s M (spanIdx s ρ) (m + 2))
1098	    (fun ρ => ∀ σ : Fin (m + 3) → ι, cechCoeff s M (spanIdx s ρ) σ)
1099	    (fun ρ => fLoc s M (spanIdx s ρ) (m + 3))
1100	    (dDiff s M (m + 1)) (dDiff s M (m + 2)) fun ρ => ?_
1101	  rw [map_dDiff_eq_locDiff s M (spanIdx s ρ) (m + 1),
1102	    map_dDiff_eq_locDiff s M (spanIdx s ρ) (m + 2)]
1103	  exact locDiff_exact s M (spanIdx s ρ) m
1104	
1105	end SectionCechModule
1106	
1107	end AlgebraicGeometry

509	\subsection{The categorical--module bridge (L1)}
510	\label{sec:cech_l1_bridge}
511	
512	The affine vanishing of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine} is a statement about the
513	\emph{section} {\v C}ech complex \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U},
514	\mathcal{F})\) of Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex} --- a cochain complex of
515	abelian groups --- whereas the combinatorial contracting-homotopy core operates on a
516	concrete complex of localised \(R\)-modules. The passage between the two, the
517	\emph{categorical--module bridge}, decomposes into two self-contained statements: the
518	section identification (each section group is an away localisation, with the
519	restriction maps the canonical localisation maps) and the homology-to-exactness
520	reduction (vanishing of the abstract homology is equivalent to exactness of the
521	underlying localised-module complex). These are isolated as
522	Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized} and
523	Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_homology_exact} below; the assembly --- feeding each
524	positive-degree node through the local-to-global exactness criterion and closing with
525	the dependent combinatorial port --- is carried out in the proof of
526	Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine}.
527	
528	\begin{definition}
529	[Quasi-coherent sections over a basic open are an away localisation]
530	  \label{def:qcoh_sections_localized}
531	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.qcohSectionsAwayLocalized}
532	  \uses{def:section_cech_complex, def:standard_affine_cover}
533	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Schemes, Lemma \texttt{lemma-spec-sheaves} (Tag 01HV),
534	  % items (4)--(5) (read from references/stacks-schemes.tex, L701--707).
535	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "(4) For every $f\in R$ we have $\Gamma(D(f), \widetilde M) = M_f$
536	  % as an $R_f$-module.
537	  % (5) Whenever $D(g) \subset D(f)$ the restriction mappings
538	  % on $\mathcal{O}_{\Spec(R)}$ and $\widetilde M$
539	  % are the maps
540	  % $R_f \to R_g$ and $M_f \to M_g$ from Lemma
541	  % \ref{lemma-standard-open}."
542	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Schemes, \texttt{lemma-spec-sheaves} (Tag 01HV),
543	  items (4)--(5).}
544	  Let \(R\) be a commutative ring, \(\mathcal{F}\) a quasi-coherent
545	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\operatorname{Spec} R}\)-module, and write
546	  \(M = \Gamma(\operatorname{Spec} R, \mathcal{F})\) for its module of global
547	  sections. For every \(g \in R\), the restriction-of-sections map
548	  \(M = \mathcal{F}(\operatorname{Spec} R) \to \mathcal{F}(D(g))\) exhibits the
549	  section group \(\mathcal{F}(D(g))\) as the away localisation \(M_g\) of \(M\); that
550	  is, this map is an \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule}\) for the multiplicative
551	  submonoid \(\operatorname{Submonoid.powers} g\) of powers of \(g\). Moreover, for
552	  \(D(g_2) \subseteq D(g_1)\) the restriction map
553	  \(\mathcal{F}(D(g_1)) \to \mathcal{F}(D(g_2))\) is, under these identifications, the
554	  canonical localisation map \(M_{g_1} \to M_{g_2}\) (the unique \(R\)-linear map
555	  compatible with the localisation units). Item~(4) of Tag~01HV gives the first
556	  assertion for \(\mathcal{F} = \widetilde{M}\) (\(\Gamma(D(g), \widetilde{M}) = M_g\)
557	  as an \(R_g\)-module) and item~(5) gives the second (the restriction along
558	  \(D(g) \subset D(f)\) is the localisation map \(M_f \to M_g\)).
559	
560	  The module-level input on the side of Mathlib is the development
561	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Tilde}: the comparison map
562	  \((\operatorname{tilde.toOpen} M\, (D(g))).\mathrm{hom}\) carries the instance
563	  \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule}(\operatorname{Submonoid.powers} g)\), and
564	  \(\operatorname{isUnit\_algebraMap\_end\_basicOpen}\) records that \(g\) acts
565	  invertibly on the sections over \(D(g)\); the restriction compatibility is
566	  \(\operatorname{tilde.toOpen\_res}\). These supply the statement \emph{verbatim} for
567	  the standard sheaf \(\widetilde{M}\). For an \emph{arbitrary} quasi-coherent
568	  \(\mathcal{F}\) the remaining step is the canonical comparison
569	  \(\mathcal{F} \cong \widetilde{\Gamma(\operatorname{Spec} R, \mathcal{F})}\), i.e.\
570	  that the counit \(\operatorname{Scheme.Modules.fromTildeΓ}\) is an isomorphism. This
571	  is \emph{not} yet available in Mathlib for a general quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{F}\):
572	  Mathlib provides it only from a \emph{global} presentation
573	  (\(\operatorname{isIso\_fromTildeΓ\_of\_presentation}\)), whereas quasi-coherence
574	  supplies only \emph{local} presentation data on a cover. Globalising the local data
575	  on the affine \(\operatorname{Spec} R\) --- the affine equivalence
576	  \(\operatorname{QCoh}(\operatorname{Spec} R) \simeq \operatorname{Mod}(R)\), Stacks
577	  Tag~01I8 --- is the one genuine gap this definition records as a build target; it is
578	  the canonical missing affine equivalence and is self-contained.
579	\end{definition}

581	\begin{lemma}
582	[Section {\v C}ech homology vanishes iff the localised complex is exact]
583	  \label{lem:section_cech_homology_exact}

585	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.Inverts,
586	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.Inverts.isUnit_powers,
587	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.Inverts.mul,
588	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.Inverts.of_dvd,
589	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.comparison,
590	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.comparison_apply,
591	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.comparison_comp,
592	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.comparison_comp_apply,
593	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.comparison_comp_structure,
594	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.comparison_self,
595	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.comparison_unique,
596	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.cechCoeff,
597	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.cechCoeff_transport_eq_comparison,
598	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.cechCoface,
599	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.cechLocalized_exact,
600	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.cechPrepend,
601	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.cech_hcomm,
602	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.cech_hsh,
603	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.cech_hu,
604	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.sprod,
605	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.sprod_cons,
606	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.sprod_succAbove_dvd}
607	  \uses{def:section_cech_complex, def:qcoh_sections_localized, lem:section_cech_module_exact}
608	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
609	  % \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (the {\v C}ech complex is
610	  % the complex of localisations) (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex,
611	  % L58--67).
612	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Clearly the {\v C}ech complex
613	  % $\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})$
614	  % is identified with the complex
615	  % $$
616	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to
617	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}} \to
618	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1i_2} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}f_{i_2}} \to
619	  % \ldots
620	  % $$"
621	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
622	  \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (identification with the
623	  complex of localisations).}
624	  Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_{\operatorname{Spec}
625	  R}\)-module and \(\mathcal{U}\) the standard cover associated with a spanning family
626	  \(s : \iota \to R\). Under the section identification of
627	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized}, the section {\v C}ech complex
628	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\) of
629	  Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex} is isomorphic, as a cochain complex, to
630	  the concrete complex of localised \(R\)-modules
631	  \[
632	    D^\bullet : \quad
633	    \prod_{\sigma : \{0\} \to \iota} M_{s_\sigma} \;\longrightarrow\;
634	    \prod_{\sigma : \{0,1\} \to \iota} M_{s_\sigma} \;\longrightarrow\;
635	    \cdots,
636	    \qquad
637	    s_\sigma = \prod_{k} s_{\sigma(k)},
638	  \]
639	  whose differential is the alternating sum of the canonical localisation maps. For
640	  each \(p\), vanishing of the abstract homology is equivalent to exactness of the
641	  underlying \(R\)-module maps,
642	  \[
643	    \operatorname{IsZero}\bigl(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U},
644	      \mathcal{F}).\mathrm{homology}\, p\bigr)
645	    \;\Longleftrightarrow\;
646	    \operatorname{Function.Exact}\bigl(D^{p-1} \to D^p \to D^{p+1}\bigr).
647	  \]
648	  The two directions are supplied by Mathlib: \(\operatorname{IsZero}\) of the
649	  homology is \(\operatorname{ExactAt}\) at \(p\)
650	  (\(\operatorname{exactAt\_iff\_isZero\_homology}\)), and \(\operatorname{ExactAt}\)
651	  of a short complex of \(R\)-modules is \(\operatorname{Function.Exact}\) of the
652	  underlying linear maps (\(\operatorname{ShortComplex.moduleCat\_exact\_iff}\)).
653	\end{lemma}
654	
655	\begin{proof}
656	  \uses{def:qcoh_sections_localized, def:section_cech_complex}
657	  The term identification is the degreewise statement of
658	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized}: the degree-\(p\) section group
659	  \(\prod_\sigma \mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) is \(\prod_\sigma M_{s_\sigma}\), since
660	  each \((p+1)\)-fold intersection \(D(s_{\sigma(0)}) \cap \cdots \cap
661	  D(s_{\sigma(p)})\) is the basic open \(D(s_\sigma)\) of the product
662	  \(s_\sigma = \prod_k s_{\sigma(k)}\) and the sections there are the away
663	  localisation \(M_{s_\sigma}\). The differential identification is item~(5) of
664	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized}: each face restriction
665	  \(\mathcal{F}(D(s_{\sigma \circ d_j})) \to \mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) is the
666	  canonical localisation map \(M_{s_{\sigma \circ d_j}} \to M_{s_\sigma}\), so the
667	  alternating sum of restrictions is the alternating sum of localisation maps, the
668	  differential of \(D^\bullet\). This gives the isomorphism of cochain complexes
669	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) \cong D^\bullet\). The
670	  homology equivalence then follows by transporting along this isomorphism and
671	  applying \(\operatorname{exactAt\_iff\_isZero\_homology}\) (\(\operatorname{IsZero}\)
672	  homology \(\Leftrightarrow\) \(\operatorname{ExactAt}\)) and
673	  \(\operatorname{ShortComplex.moduleCat\_exact\_iff}\) (\(\operatorname{ExactAt}\) of
674	  \(R\)-modules \(\Leftrightarrow\) \(\operatorname{Function.Exact}\)).
675	
676	  The identification of the abstract section complex with the concrete localised
677	  complex \(D^\bullet\) --- together with the construction of the prepend transport
678	  \(c\) and the localisation cofaces \(\delta_k\) discharging the unit, shift, and
679	  coface-commutation compatibilities \(\mathtt{hu}\)/\(\mathtt{hsh}\)/\(\mathtt{hcomm}\)
680	  of the dependent combinatorial port --- is the bulk of the bridge. The non-circular
681	  route is reflection along the localisation equivalence on its essential image (the
682	  global-sections functor \(\Gamma\) is the inverse equivalence there, so it reflects
683	  \(\operatorname{IsZero}\) of homology); it is \emph{not} the assertion that
684	  ``\(\Gamma\) preserves homology'', which would presuppose the very affine exactness
685	  being proved.
686	\end{proof}

688	\begin{definition}
689	[The un-localised section {\v C}ech module complex \(D^\bullet\)]
690	  \label{def:section_cech_module_complex}
691	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.dCoeff,
692	    AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.dCoface,
693	    AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.dDiff,
694	    AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.dDiff_apply,
695	    AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.dToCech,
696	    AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.dToCech_isLocalizedModule,
697	    AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.cechCoface_dToCech,
698	    AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.dToCech_comm,
699	    AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.cechCofaceLin,
700	    AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.cechCoface_apply,
701	    AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.locDiff,
702	    AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.locDiff_apply,
703	    AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.locDiff_eq_depDiff,
704	    AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.locDiff_exact,
705	    AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.fLoc,
706	    AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.fLoc_apply,
707	    AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.fLoc_isLocalizedModule,
708	    AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.locDiff_fLoc,
709	    AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.map_dDiff_eq_locDiff,
710	    AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.spanIdx,
711	    AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.spanIdx_spec,
712	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.comparison_isLocalizedModule,
713	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.Inverts.smul_pow_cancel}
714	  \uses{def:standard_affine_cover, def:qcoh_sections_localized}
715	  Let \(R\) be a commutative ring, \(M = \Gamma(\operatorname{Spec} R, \mathcal{F})\)
716	  the global sections of a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_{\operatorname{Spec}
717	  R}\)-module \(\mathcal{F}\), and \(s : \iota \to R\) (\(\iota\) finite) a spanning
718	  family of the unit ideal, \(\operatorname{Ideal.span}(\operatorname{range} s) =
719	  \top\) (Definition~\ref{def:standard_affine_cover}). The \emph{un-localised section
720	  {\v C}ech module complex} \(D^\bullet\) is the cochain complex of \(R\)-modules
721	  whose degree-\(p\) term is the product over multi-indices
722	  \(\sigma : \{0, \ldots, p\} \to \iota\) of the away localisations
723	  \[
724	    D^p \;=\; \prod_{\sigma} M_{s_\sigma},
725	    \qquad s_\sigma = \prod_{k} s_{\sigma(k)},
726	    \qquad M_{s_\sigma} = \operatorname{LocalizedModule}
727	      \bigl(\operatorname{Submonoid.powers}(s_\sigma)\bigr)\, M,
728	  \]
729	  with differential \(d^p : D^p \to D^{p+1}\) the alternating sum
730	  \(\sum_{j} (-1)^j\, \delta^j\) of the coface comparison maps \(\delta^j\), each of
731	  which is the canonical away-localisation comparison
732	  \(M_{s_{\sigma \circ d_j}} \to M_{s_\sigma}\) sending a fraction to its image under
733	  the further localisation at the omitted factor. The single-coefficient comparison
734	  map and its localisation property are recorded by the \(\mathtt{dCoface}\) and
735	  \(\mathtt{dCoeff}\) data and the differential by \(\mathtt{dDiff}\) (with action
736	  \(\mathtt{dDiff\_apply}\)); the auxiliary \(\mathtt{spanIdx}\)/\(\mathtt{spanIdx\_spec}\)
737	  select a witnessing index of the spanning relation.
738	
739	  The complex \(D^\bullet\) is tied to the section {\v C}ech complex of
740	  Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex} and to its localisations by three
741	  comparison families. First, the maps \(\mathtt{dToCech}\) identify each term
742	  \(D^p\) with the degree-\(p\) section group
743	  \(\prod_\sigma \mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) of
744	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized} as a localised module
745	  (\(\mathtt{dToCech\_isLocalizedModule}\)), intertwining the differentials
746	  (\(\mathtt{cechCoface\_dToCech}\), \(\mathtt{dToCech\_comm}\)); the section-side
747	  cofaces are \(\mathtt{cechCofaceLin}\) with action \(\mathtt{cechCoface\_apply}\).
748	  Second, localising \(D^\bullet\) at a spanning element \(s_r\) reproduces the
749	  \emph{localised} {\v C}ech complex: the localised differential \(\mathtt{locDiff}\)
750	  (action \(\mathtt{locDiff\_apply}\)) agrees, via
751	  \(\mathtt{locDiff\_eq\_depDiff}\), with the dependent-coefficient combinatorial
752	  differential, and its exactness is recorded as \(\mathtt{locDiff\_exact}\). The
753	  localisation comparison maps \(\mathtt{fLoc}\) (action \(\mathtt{fLoc\_apply}\))
754	  exhibit each localised term as a localised module
755	  (\(\mathtt{fLoc\_isLocalizedModule}\)) intertwining \(\mathtt{dDiff}\) with
756	  \(\mathtt{locDiff}\) (\(\mathtt{locDiff\_fLoc}\), \(\mathtt{map\_dDiff\_eq\_locDiff}\)).
757	  The key algebraic fact underlying all three families is the localisation
758	  transitivity \(M_a[1/b] = M_{ab}\): localising the away module \(M_{s_\sigma}\)
759	  further at \(s_r\) gives \(M_{s_r s_\sigma}\). This is the keystone
760	  \(\mathtt{AwayComparison.comparison\_isLocalizedModule}\) (the composite of two away
761	  localisations is again the away localisation at the product), with the cancellation
762	  bookkeeping \(\mathtt{AwayComparison.Inverts.smul\_pow\_cancel}\). Thus localising
763	  \(D^\bullet\) at \(s_r\) (an \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule.Away}\)) reproduces
764	  the localised Čech complex of Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_homology_exact}, and
765	  exactness of \(D^\bullet\) can be checked one localisation at a time.
766	\end{definition}

768	\begin{lemma}
769	[Positive-degree exactness of the section {\v C}ech module complex]
770	  \label{lem:section_cech_module_exact}
771	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.dDiff_exact}
772	  \uses{def:section_cech_module_complex}
773	  With the notation of Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_module_complex}, the
774	  un-localised section {\v C}ech module complex \(D^\bullet\) is exact in positive
775	  degrees: for every \(p\),
776	  \[
777	    \operatorname{Function.Exact}\bigl(d^{p+1} : D^{p+1} \to D^{p+2},\;
778	      d^{p+2} : D^{p+2} \to D^{p+3}\bigr).
779	  \]
780	  This is step~(a) of the affine vanishing: the node-by-node exactness of the
781	  un-localised module complex, from which the homology vanishing of the section
782	  {\v C}ech complex (Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_homology_exact}, step~(c)) is read off.
783	\end{lemma}
784	
785	\begin{proof}
786	  \uses{def:section_cech_module_complex}
787	  Exactness of \(D^\bullet\) at each positive-degree node is a local-to-global
788	  statement over the spanning family. By the local-to-global exactness criterion
789	  \(\operatorname{exact\_of\_isLocalized\_span}(\operatorname{Set.range} s)\) (with the
790	  spanning hypothesis \(\operatorname{Ideal.span}(\operatorname{range} s) = \top\)), it
791	  suffices to verify \(\operatorname{Function.Exact}(d^{p+1}, d^{p+2})\) after
792	  localising at each spanning element \(s_r\). In the localisation at \(s_r\) the
793	  comparison family \(\mathtt{map\_dDiff\_eq\_locDiff}\) identifies the localised
794	  differential of \(D^\bullet\) with the localised Čech differential \(\mathtt{locDiff}\)
795	  of Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_module_complex} (using the localisation
796	  transitivity \(M_a[1/b] = M_{ab}\) of
797	  \(\mathtt{AwayComparison.comparison\_isLocalizedModule}\)). Exactness of that
798	  localised differential is \(\mathtt{locDiff\_exact}\), which is in turn the
799	  dependent-coefficient combinatorial exactness transported along
800	  \(\mathtt{locDiff\_eq\_depDiff}\); the contracting homotopy at the fixed index
801	  \(r\) (where \(s_r\) is invertible) furnishes \((dh + hd) = \operatorname{id}\),
802	  giving the localised exactness one localisation at a time. Reassembling over the
803	  spanning family yields the un-localised exactness.
804	\end{proof}

905	% This proves the identity map is homotopic to zero as desired."
906	\begin{proof}
907	  \uses{def:section_cech_complex, def:qcoh_sections_localized, lem:section_cech_homology_exact, lem:section_cech_module_exact}
908	  Write \(R\) for the ring, \(M = \Gamma(\operatorname{Spec} R, \mathcal{F})\) for the
909	  global sections, and \((s : \iota \to R,\; hs : \operatorname{Ideal.span}
910	  (\operatorname{range} s) = \top)\) for the spanning family of
911	  Definition~\ref{def:standard_affine_cover}.
912	
913	  \paragraph{Reduction to the localised complex.}
914	  By the section identification of Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized}, each
915	  \((p+1)\)-fold intersection \(D(s_{\sigma(0)}) \cap \cdots \cap D(s_{\sigma(p)})\)
916	  is the basic open \(D(s_\sigma)\) of the product
917	  \(s_\sigma = \prod_{k} s_{\sigma(k)}\), and its sections are the away localisation
918	  \(\mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma)) = M_{s_\sigma}\); under these identifications each face
919	  restriction is the canonical localisation map. Hence
920	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_homology_exact} identifies the section {\v C}ech complex
921	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\) of
922	  Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex} with the concrete complex of localised
923	  \(R\)-modules
924	  \[
925	    \prod_{i_0} M_{s_{i_0}} \;\to\;
926	    \prod_{i_0 i_1} M_{s_{i_0} s_{i_1}} \;\to\;
927	    \prod_{i_0 i_1 i_2} M_{s_{i_0} s_{i_1} s_{i_2}} \;\to\; \cdots,
928	  \]
929	  and reduces the vanishing
930	  \(\operatorname{IsZero}(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U},
931	  \mathcal{F}).\mathrm{homology}\, p)\) for \(p > 0\) to positive-degree
932	  \(\operatorname{Function.Exact}\) of the underlying localisation maps. Equivalently,
933	  the extended complex \(0 \to M \to \prod_{i_0} M_{s_{i_0}} \to \cdots\) (whose
934	  truncation is studied in Algebra, Lemma \texttt{algebra-lemma-cover-module}) is
935	  exact in positive degrees.
936	
937	  \paragraph{Node-by-node exactness via local-to-global and the dependent port.}
938	  Exactness of each positive-degree node is established from the spanning family
939	  \((s, hs)\). The local-to-global exactness criterion
940	  \(\operatorname{exact\_of\_isLocalized\_span}(\operatorname{Set.range} s)\, hs\)
941	  reduces \(\operatorname{Function.Exact}(d^{p-1}, d^{p})\) to exactness after
942	  localising at each spanning element \(r\) (the away localisation
943	  \(\operatorname{Away}(s_r)\)), checking exactness one localisation at a time. In the
944	  localisation at \(s_r\) the fixed index \(i_{\mathrm{fix}} = r\) makes \(s_r\)
945	  invertible, so the prepend-\(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\) transport \(c\)
946	  (\(\operatorname{depTransport}\)) is defined globally on that localisation and
947	  furnishes a contracting homotopy of the localised complex: the unit, shift, and
948	  coface-commutation compatibilities \(\mathtt{hu}\) (\(c \circ \delta_0 =
949	  \operatorname{id}\)), \(\mathtt{hsh}\) (\(c \circ \delta_{k+1} = \delta_k \circ c\)),
950	  and \(\mathtt{hcomm}\) hold for the localisation cofaces \(\delta_k\). The node is
951	  then closed by \(\operatorname{depDiff\_exact}\) of the dependent combinatorial port,
952	  which packages the identity \((dh + hd) = \operatorname{id}\) into the
953	  \(\operatorname{Function.Exact}\) statement that
954	  \(\operatorname{exact\_of\_isLocalized\_span}\) consumes. The constant- and
955	  dependent-coefficient combinatorial cores and the spanning-family certifier are
956	  already in place; the section and differential identifications of
957	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized} and the homology--exactness reduction of
958	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_homology_exact} are the bridge that connects them to the
959	  abstract section complex.
960	
961	  The homotopy is built directly from the away-localisation identification, \emph{not}
962	  routed through \(\operatorname{SimplicialObject.Augmented.ExtraDegeneracy}\): the
963	  extra-degeneracy packaging of the contracting homotopy is a documented dead end for
964	  this cover (the prepend-\(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\) map is only a section-level homotopy
965	  after localisation, not an augmented-simplicial extra degeneracy), so the concrete
966	  \(\operatorname{depHomotopy}\)/\(\operatorname{depDiff}\) route of the dependent port
967	  is used instead.
968	\end{proof}

290	/-- **Cosimplicial abelian group underlying the section {\v C}ech complex.**
291	
292	For a family of opens `U : ι → Opens X` (an open covering) and a presheaf of modules `F`,
293	the cosimplicial object in `Ab` whose value in cosimplicial degree `p` is the product
294	`∏_{σ : Fin (p+1) → ι} F(⨅ₖ U (σ k))` of sections over the `(p+1)`-fold intersections. A
295	cosimplicial map `f : [m] ⟶ [n]` acts at multi-index `σ : Fin (n+1) → ι` by projecting to
296	the coordinate `σ ∘ f` and then restricting along `⨅ₖ U (σ k) ≤ ⨅ₗ U (σ (f l))`. This is the
297	covariant ({\v C}ech) variance that the alternating-coface-map complex turns into a cochain
298	complex.
299	
300	Project-local: Mathlib has no {\v C}ech complex of sections of a presheaf of modules. Built
301	as an honest `SimplexCategory ⥤ Ab` functor so that `alternatingCofaceMapComplex` supplies
302	`d² = 0` for free (no hand-rolled alternating-sum identity). -/
303	noncomputable def sectionCechCosimplicial {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
304	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) : CosimplicialObject Ab.{u} where
305	  obj n := ∏ᶜ (fun σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → ι => F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k))))
306	  map {m n} f := Pi.lift (fun σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → ι =>
307	    Pi.π _ (σ ∘ f.toOrderHom) ≫
308	      F.presheaf.map (homOfLE (le_iInf (fun l => iInf_le _ (f.toOrderHom l)))).op)
309	  map_id n := by
310	    apply Pi.hom_ext
311	    intro σ
312	    simp only [Pi.lift_π, Category.id_comp, SimplexCategory.id_toOrderHom,
313	      OrderHom.id_coe, Function.comp_id]
314	    rw [Subsingleton.elim (homOfLE _).op (𝟙 _)]
315	    simp
316	  map_comp {l m n} f g := by
317	    apply Pi.hom_ext
318	    intro σ
319	    simp only [Pi.lift_π, Category.assoc, SimplexCategory.comp_toOrderHom, OrderHom.comp_coe]
320	    rw [← Category.assoc, Pi.lift_π, Category.assoc, ← Functor.map_comp]
321	    congr 1
322	
323	/-- **Section {\v C}ech cochain complex of a presheaf of modules**
324	(blueprint `def:section_cech_complex`).
325	
326	The cochain complex `Č•(𝒰, F)` of abelian groups with degree-`p` term
327	`∏_{σ : Fin (p+1) → ι} F(⨅ₖ U (σ k))` and the alternating-sum-of-restrictions differential,
328	obtained as the alternating-coface-map complex of `sectionCechCosimplicial`. This is the
329	section complex of the blueprint, kept distinct from the relative pushforward `CechComplex`;
330	it is the right-hand side of the {\v C}ech hom-identification
331	`Hom_{PMod}(K(𝒰)_•, F) ≅ Č•(𝒰, F)`.
332	
333	Project-local: no {\v C}ech complex of sections of a presheaf of modules exists in Mathlib. -/
334	noncomputable def sectionCechComplex {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
335	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) : CochainComplex Ab.{u} ℕ :=
336	  (AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex Ab.{u}).obj (sectionCechCosimplicial U F)
337	
338	end AlgebraicGeometry

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — quasi-coherent sections as away localisations (L1, step (b))

The categorical→module bridge needs the section-identification of
`def:qcoh_sections_localized`: over a basic open `D(g)` the sections of a
quasi-coherent sheaf are the away localisation `M_g`, and the restriction maps
between basic opens are the canonical localisation comparison maps.  For the
standard sheaf `tilde M` this is *verbatim* from Mathlib's `Tilde` development
(`AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen` carries `IsLocalizedModule (.powers g)` and the
restriction compatibility is `tilde.toOpen_res`); the only project-local content is
(i) the multi-index intersection `⨅ₖ D(s_{σ k}) = D(s_σ)` identification, which lets
the degree-`p` section group over the `(p+1)`-fold intersection be read as the
localisation `M_{s_σ}` (this is what `lem:section_cech_homology_exact` consumes
degreewise), and (ii) the identification of the abstract presheaf restriction with
`AwayComparison.comparison`, the differential brick.

For an *arbitrary* quasi-coherent `F` the remaining input is the affine equivalence
`F ≅ tilde(ΓF)` (Stacks 01I8); see `def:qcoh_sections_localized`.  The tilde case
below is the gap-free part that lands the named target. -/

/-- Splitting an indexed infimum over `Fin (n+1)` into the `0`-th term and the
infimum over the tail.  Lattice-combinatorial helper behind `basicOpen_sprod`. -/
private lemma iInf_fin_succ {α : Type*} [CompleteLattice α] (n : ℕ) (f : Fin (n + 1) → α) :
    (⨅ i, f i) = f 0 ⊓ ⨅ i : Fin n, f i.succ := by
  apply le_antisymm
  · exact le_inf (iInf_le _ 0) (le_iInf fun i => iInf_le _ i.succ)
  · refine le_iInf fun i => ?_
    refine Fin.cases inf_le_left (fun j => le_trans inf_le_right (iInf_le _ j)) i

/-- **Multi-index basic-open intersection** (geometric input to step (c)): the
`(p+1)`-fold intersection of the basic opens `D(s_{σ k})` of a {\v C}ech multi-index
`σ : Fin n → ι` is the basic open `D(s_σ)` of the product `s_σ = ∏ₖ s_{σ k}`.  This
identifies the section group `F(⨅ₖ D(s_{σ k}))` of `sectionCechCosimplicial` with the
away localisation `M_{s_σ}` of `SectionCechModule.dCoeff`. -/
lemma basicOpen_sprod {R : CommRingCat.{u}} {ι : Type u} (n : ℕ) (s : ι → R) (σ : Fin n → ι) :
    (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)) : (Spec R).Opens)
      = PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (∏ k, s (σ k)) := by
  induction n with
  | zero =>
      rw [show (∏ k, s (σ k)) = 1 from Finset.prod_of_isEmpty _]
      simp only [PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen_one]
      exact iInf_of_empty _
  | succ m ih =>
      rw [Fin.prod_univ_succ, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen_mul, ← ih (fun i => σ i.succ),
        iInf_fin_succ]
      rfl

/-- **Quasi-coherent sections over a {\v C}ech intersection are an away localisation**
(`def:qcoh_sections_localized`, tilde case — step (b) of the L1 bridge).  For the
standard sheaf `tilde M` of an `R`-module `M`, the section-restriction map from `M`
to the sections over the `(p+1)`-fold basic-open intersection
`⨅ₖ D(s_{σ k}) = D(s_σ)` exhibits that section group as the away localisation
`M_{s_σ}` (`IsLocalizedModule` for `Submonoid.powers (∏ₖ s_{σ k})`).  Combines the
Mathlib instance `IsLocalizedModule (.powers g) (tilde.toOpen M (D g)).hom` with the
intersection identification `basicOpen_sprod`.  This is the degreewise section
identification that `lem:section_cech_homology_exact` consumes; the restriction
compatibility (item (5) of the blueprint definition) is `tilde.toOpen_res` together
with `qcohRestriction_eq_comparison` below. -/
lemma qcohSectionsAwayLocalized {R : CommRingCat.{u}} {ι : Type u}
    (M : ModuleCat.{u} R) {n : ℕ} (s : ι → R) (σ : Fin n → ι) :
    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (∏ k, s (σ k)))
      (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen M (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)))).hom := by
  rw [basicOpen_sprod]
  infer_instance

/-- **Restriction = localisation comparison** (`def:qcoh_sections_localized`, item (5);
the differential brick of step (c)).  For the standard sheaf `tilde M`, the presheaf
restriction map between basic-open section groups `M_a → M_b` (along an inclusion
`D(b) ⊆ D(a)`) is, as an `R`-linear map, the canonical away-localisation comparison
`AwayComparison.comparison` — provided `a` acts invertibly on `M_b` (`Inverts a M_b`,
which holds whenever `a ∣ b`, the {\v C}ech-face case).  Proved by the universal
property `AwayComparison.comparison_unique`: both the restriction and the comparison
are `R`-linear maps that recover `tilde.toOpen M (D b)` after precomposition with
`tilde.toOpen M (D a)` (the restriction does so by `tilde.toOpen_res`).  Summed over
the alternating signs, this identifies the section {\v C}ech differential with the
module differential `SectionCechModule.dDiff`. -/
lemma qcohRestriction_eq_comparison {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (M : ModuleCat.{u} R) {a b : R}
    (i : (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b : (Spec R).Opens) ⟶ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a)
    (hb : AwayComparison.Inverts a
      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).presheaf.obj
        (Opposite.op (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b)))) :
    ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).presheaf.map i.op).hom
      = AwayComparison.comparison (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a)).hom
          (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b)).hom hb := by
  refine (AwayComparison.comparison_unique _ _ hb _ ?_).symm
  have h := AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen_res M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a)
    (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b) i
  exact congrArg ModuleCat.Hom.hom h

end AlgebraicGeometry

Even if you think you know the API — search anyway. Signatures change, instances disappear.

### 3. Build bottom-up, step by step

Start from the deepest missing piece identified in your scouting. For each step:

1. **Draft the type signature** and check it typechecks via `lean_diagnostic_messages` or `lean_run_code` before attempting the body.
2. **Write the proof.** Use Mathlib lemmas you verified exist. If a sub-step is missing, recurse: prove that sub-step first.

4. **Continue to the next step** in the chain.

Push as far as you can. Each axiom-clean step you add is permanent progress.

### 4. When stuck on a step — try before stopping

Before declaring a step impossible:

1. **Try a different proof route.** Reformulate the statement, weaken to a special case you can prove, use a detour through an equivalent form.
2. **Use the informal agent** if an API key is available (check `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` first): `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` "Prove [goal] using only current Mathlib." Formalize whatever it suggests. If no key is set, skip to step 3.
3. **Search more broadly.** The lemma you need might exist under a different name or in a namespace you haven't checked.
4. **Prove a strictly smaller sub-step** that is axiom-clean and genuinely useful — something that shrinks the remaining gap.

Only after exhausting these alternatives do you stop. **Comments describing an approach you haven't tried are not exhausted alternatives — attempt them first.**

### 5. Stopping

**Before stopping, run this self-review:**

Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` (mirror the `.lean` path):

```markdown
# Algebra/MathlibSupplement.lean

## Session summary
- Built axiom-clean: `Foo.bar`, `Foo.baz` (lines 12–40)
- Blocked on: `Foo.qux` — needs `Bar.aux` which requires [precise statement]

## Foo.bar (line 12)
- **Approach:** Direct from `Mathlib.Algebra.X` via `simp [Y]`
- **Result:** RESOLVED — axiom-clean

## Foo.qux (not added)
- **Approach 1:** Tried [route] — FAILED because [reason]
- **Approach 2:** Tried informal agent — suggested [sketch], formalization blocked on [specific gap]
- **Next step:** Build `Bar.aux : [precise type statement]` first
- **Dead end:** Do not retry [route] — [why it fails]
```

One section per declaration attempted. For each: approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / PARTIAL / NOT ADDED), dead-end warnings, next step.

## End-of-session handoff

**Write a `## Summary` section** before the verdicts: declarations added (count + names), declarations blocked (count + why), sorry count before → after across your file.

**Write a `## Needs blueprint entry` section.** This mode is the biggest source of blueprint debt: every non-private definition/lemma you add is, by construction, infrastructure with **no blueprint block yet** — a `lean_aux` node invisible to the dependency graph (`archon dag-query unmatched` will list it) until the planner/reviewer blueprints it. List each new non-private declaration (name, file, and the facts its proof relies on) so the 1-to-1 Lean ↔ blueprint correspondence the dag agent built is restored next iter. Closing 0 project sorries while adding 3 unreported helpers does not advance the project — it quietly re-introduces the isolation the DAG phase worked to eliminate. Reporting them is mandatory.

**Write a `## Why I stopped` section** — be brutally honest. Only axiom-clean Lean declarations count as progress. Comments, sketches, and log prose do NOT. If you wrote down approaches you didn't attempt, say so:

- `Real progress`: N axiom-clean declarations added — name each one with its line number.
- `Partial progress`: added some declarations, hit a specific blocker on the next — name the blocker precisely (not "it's complex", but the exact type or missing ingredient).
- `Approaches written but not attempted`: identified routes in comments or log but did not attempt them — name each. This is valid but weak; the planner will re-assign with a directive to attempt them.
- `Blocked — alternatives exhausted`: informal agent called (or key unavailable, documented), alternative routes tried, all failed — name what was tried, why each failed, and the exact type statement of the next needed ingredient.
- `Infrastructure already exists`: Mathlib has what was needed — cite the exact lemma name and namespace.

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi (OpenAI-compatible via `--provider kimi`), Kimi (Anthropic-compatible via `--provider kimi-anthropic`), OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. Both `kimi` and `kimi-anthropic` use the same `MOONSHOT_API_KEY`; the latter speaks Moonshot's Anthropic-compatible Messages endpoint and supports `--think`. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | read | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | **write** (shared notice board) | **write** (shared notice board) | **write** (shared notice board) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	Archon talks back to the user through **`TO_USER.md`** — a single, *persistent* notice board surfaced as a UI banner. It is **shared and writable by every agent** (plan, prover, review). It is no longer cleared at the start of each review phase; content survives across iters until an agent prunes it. The discipline every writer follows:
112	
113	- **Concise**: the whole file stays ≤ 2–3 short bullets. It is a banner, not a log.
114	- **Relevant now**: before adding anything, read the current content and **delete any bullet that is no longer true at this point** (the blocker was resolved, the decision was overtaken, the credential got set). Never let it accumulate stale notices.
115	- **Notice board, never a question queue**: the loop is autonomous and may run unattended for many iters. Never write "awaiting your decision", an options menu, a "where to reply", or "default to X if no reply" — anything implying the loop is paused for a human. State decisions as *made* and blockers as *worked-around-as-far-as-possible*; the user steers asynchronously via `USER_HINTS.md`.
116	
117	## Agent Roles
118	
119	### Plan Agent
120	
121	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
122	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
123	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
124	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
125	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
126	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
127	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
128	
129	### Prover Agent
130	
131	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
132	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.
133	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.
134	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
135	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
136	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
137	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
138	
139	### Subagents
140	
141	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
142	
143	### Review Agent
144	
145	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
146	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
147	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
148	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
149	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
150	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
151	
152	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
153	
154	These run automatically between phases:
155	
156	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
157	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
158	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.
