621	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
622	  \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (identification with the
623	  complex of localisations).}
624	  Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_{\operatorname{Spec}
625	  R}\)-module and \(\mathcal{U}\) the standard cover associated with a spanning family
626	  \(s : \iota \to R\). Under the section identification of
627	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized}, the section {\v C}ech complex
628	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\) of
629	  Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex} is isomorphic, as a cochain complex, to
630	  the concrete complex of localised \(R\)-modules
631	  \[
632	    D^\bullet : \quad
633	    \prod_{\sigma : \{0\} \to \iota} M_{s_\sigma} \;\longrightarrow\;
634	    \prod_{\sigma : \{0,1\} \to \iota} M_{s_\sigma} \;\longrightarrow\;
635	    \cdots,
636	    \qquad
637	    s_\sigma = \prod_{k} s_{\sigma(k)},
638	  \]
639	  whose differential is the alternating sum of the canonical localisation maps. For
640	  each \(p\), vanishing of the abstract homology is equivalent to exactness of the
641	  underlying \(R\)-module maps,
642	  \[
643	    \operatorname{IsZero}\bigl(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U},
644	      \mathcal{F}).\mathrm{homology}\, p\bigr)
645	    \;\Longleftrightarrow\;
646	    \operatorname{Function.Exact}\bigl(D^{p-1} \to D^p \to D^{p+1}\bigr).
647	  \]
648	  The two directions are supplied by Mathlib: \(\operatorname{IsZero}\) of the
649	  homology is \(\operatorname{ExactAt}\) at \(p\)
650	  (\(\operatorname{exactAt\_iff\_isZero\_homology}\)), and \(\operatorname{ExactAt}\)
651	  of a short complex of \(R\)-modules is \(\operatorname{Function.Exact}\) of the
652	  underlying linear maps (\(\operatorname{ShortComplex.moduleCat\_exact\_iff}\)).
653	\end{lemma}
654	
655	\begin{proof}
656	  \uses{def:qcoh_sections_localized, def:section_cech_complex}
657	  The term identification is the degreewise statement of
658	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized}: the degree-\(p\) section group
659	  \(\prod_\sigma \mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) is \(\prod_\sigma M_{s_\sigma}\), since
660	  each \((p+1)\)-fold intersection \(D(s_{\sigma(0)}) \cap \cdots \cap
661	  D(s_{\sigma(p)})\) is the basic open \(D(s_\sigma)\) of the product
662	  \(s_\sigma = \prod_k s_{\sigma(k)}\) and the sections there are the away
663	  localisation \(M_{s_\sigma}\). The differential identification is item~(5) of
664	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized}: each face restriction
665	  \(\mathcal{F}(D(s_{\sigma \circ d_j})) \to \mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) is the
666	  canonical localisation map \(M_{s_{\sigma \circ d_j}} \to M_{s_\sigma}\), so the
667	  alternating sum of restrictions is the alternating sum of localisation maps, the
668	  differential of \(D^\bullet\). This gives the isomorphism of cochain complexes
669	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) \cong D^\bullet\). The
670	  homology equivalence then follows by transporting along this isomorphism and
671	  applying \(\operatorname{exactAt\_iff\_isZero\_homology}\) (\(\operatorname{IsZero}\)
672	  homology \(\Leftrightarrow\) \(\operatorname{ExactAt}\)) and
673	  \(\operatorname{ShortComplex.moduleCat\_exact\_iff}\) (\(\operatorname{ExactAt}\) of
674	  \(R\)-modules \(\Leftrightarrow\) \(\operatorname{Function.Exact}\)).
675	
676	  The identification of the abstract section complex with the concrete localised
677	  complex \(D^\bullet\) --- together with the construction of the prepend transport
678	  \(c\) and the localisation cofaces \(\delta_k\) discharging the unit, shift, and
679	  coface-commutation compatibilities \(\mathtt{hu}\)/\(\mathtt{hsh}\)/\(\mathtt{hcomm}\)
680	  of the dependent combinatorial port --- is the bulk of the bridge. The non-circular
681	  route is reflection along the localisation equivalence on its essential image (the
682	  global-sections functor \(\Gamma\) is the inverse equivalence there, so it reflects
683	  \(\operatorname{IsZero}\) of homology); it is \emph{not} the assertion that
684	  ``\(\Gamma\) preserves homology'', which would presuppose the very affine exactness
685	  being proved.
686	\end{proof}

1294	\begin{lemma}
1295	[Objectwise detection of quasi-isomorphisms of presheaf-of-module complexes]
1296	  \label{lem:quasiIso_of_evaluation}
1297	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.quasiIso_of_evaluation,
1298	    AlgebraicGeometry.isIso_Fmap_homologyMap,
1299	    AlgebraicGeometry.isIso_of_evaluation}
1300	  \uses{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex}
1301	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology, Lemma \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}
1302	  % (proof, the sectionwise-reduction step) (read from
1303	  % references/stacks-cohomology.tex, L1220--1226).
1304	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "It suffices to show that taking sections of this extended
1305	  % complex over any open $W \subset X$ leads to an acyclic complex."
1306	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology, \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}
1307	  (sectionwise reduction).}
1308	  Let \(R\) be a presheaf of rings on a category \(C\) and let
1309	  \(\varphi : K \to L\) be a morphism of chain complexes in the abelian category
1310	  \(\mathrm{PMod}(R)\) of presheaves of \(R\)-modules. Suppose that for \emph{every}
1311	  object \(V\) the evaluated morphism — the image of \(\varphi\) under the functor
1312	  \(\bigl(\operatorname{PresheafOfModules.evaluation} R\, V\bigr).
1313	  \operatorname{mapHomologicalComplex}\), a morphism of complexes of
1314	  \(R(V)\)-modules — is a quasi-isomorphism. Then \(\varphi\) is a
1315	  quasi-isomorphism. This is the objectwise-reduction step that turns the
1316	  resolution claim of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_complex_quasi_iso} into a family of
1317	  sectionwise (combinatorial) statements: homology in \(\mathrm{PMod}(R)\) is
1318	  computed open-by-open, because the evaluation functors
1319	  \(\operatorname{PresheafOfModules.evaluation} R\, V\) are exact (hence preserve
1320	  homology) and jointly conservative. The Lean entry point is
1321	  \(\operatorname{quasiIso\_of\_evaluation}\), which combines the joint
1322	  conservativity of the evaluations (each \(g\) is an isomorphism as soon as every
1323	  \((\operatorname{evaluation} R\, V).\operatorname{map} g\) is) with the fact that
1324	  each \(\operatorname{PresheafOfModules.evaluation} R\, V\) preserves homology.
1325	\end{lemma}
1326	
1327	\begin{proof}
1328	  \uses{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex}
1329	  A morphism of complexes is a quasi-isomorphism iff it induces an isomorphism on
1330	  homology in every degree (\(\operatorname{quasiIso\_iff}\),
1331	  \(\operatorname{quasiIsoAt\_iff\_isIso\_homologyMap}\)). Fix a degree \(i\). The
1332	  homology map \(H_i(\varphi)\) is an isomorphism in \(\mathrm{PMod}(R)\) iff each of
1333	  its evaluations is an isomorphism, since the underlying
1334	  presheaf-of-abelian-groups functor reflects isomorphisms and a morphism of
1335	  presheaves is an isomorphism iff it is so objectwise (joint conservativity). For
1336	  each \(V\), evaluation preserves homology, so the evaluation of \(H_i(\varphi)\)
1337	  is the homology map of the evaluated complex morphism; the latter is an
1338	  isomorphism because the evaluated morphism is a quasi-isomorphism by hypothesis.
1339	  Hence \(H_i(\varphi)\) is an isomorphism for all \(i\), i.e.\ \(\varphi\) is a
1340	  quasi-isomorphism.
1341	\end{proof}

1343	\begin{lemma}
1344	[Sectionwise description of the evaluated free complex]
1345	  \label{lem:cech_free_eval_sectionwise}
1346	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechFreeEval_X}
1347	  \uses{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex, def:cover_structure_presheaf}
1348	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology, Lemma \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}
1349	  % (proof, the sectionwise description of $K^{ext}_\bullet(W)$) (read from
1350	  % references/stacks-cohomology.tex, L1227--1252).
1351	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Write $I = I_1 \amalg I_2$ where $W \subset U_i$ if and only
1352	  % if $i \in I_1$. ... If $I_1 \not = \emptyset$, then
1353	  % $\mathcal{O}_\mathcal{U}(W) = \mathcal{O}_X(W)$ and
1354	  % $K^{ext}_p(W) = \bigoplus\nolimits_{i_0 \ldots i_p \in I_1} \mathcal{O}_X(W)$.
1355	  % This is true because of the simple description of the presheaves
1356	  % $(j_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_{p!}\mathcal{O}_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}}$."
1357	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology, \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}
1358	  (description of the evaluated complex).}
1359	  Fix an open \(V \subset X\) and write \(I_1 = I_1(V) = \{\,i \in I : V \leq U_i\,\}\)
1360	  for the set of cover members containing \(V\). Evaluating the free presheaf complex
1361	  \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\) of Definition~\ref{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex} at
1362	  \(V\) gives, degreewise,
1363	  \[
1364	    K(\mathcal{U})_p(V)
1365	      = \bigoplus_{\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to I}
1366	        \operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_\sigma)(V)
1367	      = \bigoplus_{\substack{\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to I \\ V \leq U_\sigma}}
1368	        \mathcal{O}_X(V)
1369	      = \bigoplus_{\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to I_1} \mathcal{O}_X(V),
1370	  \]
1371	  because \(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,W)(V) = \mathcal{O}_X(V)\) when
1372	  \(V \leq W\) and \(0\) otherwise (poset hom-set is a subsingleton), and
1373	  \(V \leq U_\sigma = U_{\sigma(0)} \cap \cdots \cap U_{\sigma(p)}\) holds iff
1374	  \(\sigma\) takes all its values in \(I_1\). Under this identification the
1375	  differential of \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet(V)\) is the alternating-sum combinatorial
1376	  {\v C}ech differential of the full simplex on the index set \(I_1\) with
1377	  coefficients in the single module \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\). Moreover the evaluated
1378	  augmentation target is \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}(V) = \mathcal{O}_X(V)\) when
1379	  \(I_1 \neq \varnothing\) and \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}(V) = 0\) when
1380	  \(I_1 = \varnothing\). Thus the evaluation at \(V\) of the augmented complex
1381	  \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet \to \mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}[0]\)
1382	  (\(\operatorname{cechFreeComplexAug}\)) is precisely the augmented combinatorial
1383	  {\v C}ech complex of the simplex on \(I_1\) with coefficients \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\).
1384	  The Lean entry point for the degree-\(p\) unfolding is
1385	  \(\operatorname{cechFreePresheafComplex\_X}\) (which exposes
1386	  \(K(\mathcal{U})_p = \coprod_\sigma \operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_\sigma)\) by
1387	  \(\operatorname{rfl}\)) composed with
1388	  \(\operatorname{PresheafOfModules.evaluation}\) at \(V\).
1389	\end{lemma}
1390	
1391	\begin{proof}
1392	  \uses{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex, def:cover_structure_presheaf}
1393	  The degreewise identity is the value at \(V\) of the explicit description of the
1394	  free presheaf \(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,W)\) recorded in
1395	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex}: it sends \(V\) to the free
1396	  \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\)-module on \(\operatorname{Hom}(V, W)\), which is
1397	  \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\) if \(V \leq W\) and \(0\) otherwise. Applying
1398	  \(\operatorname{PresheafOfModules.evaluation}\;V\) to the coproduct decomposition
1399	  \(\operatorname{cechFreePresheafComplex\_X}\) commutes with the direct sum and
1400	  collapses each summand to \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\) or \(0\) according to whether
1401	  \(V \leq U_\sigma\), i.e.\ whether \(\sigma\) factors through \(I_1\); the surviving
1402	  summands are exactly those indexed by \(\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to I_1\). The
1403	  evaluated faces are the index-dropping restriction maps, which on the surviving
1404	  summands are identities of \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\), so the alternating sum is the
1405	  combinatorial {\v C}ech differential. The augmentation value follows from
1406	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}(V) = \mathcal{O}_X(V)\) exactly when some \(U_i\)
1407	  contains \(V\) (\(I_1 \neq \varnothing\)), recorded in
1408	  Definition~\ref{def:cover_structure_presheaf}.
1409	\end{proof}

135	namespace CombinatorialCech
136	
137	variable {ι : Type*} {M : Type*} [AddCommGroup M] {n : ℕ}
138	
139	/-- Alternating coface (Čech) differential with constant coefficients in `M`:
140	`Cⁿ = (Fin n → ι) → M ⟶ Cⁿ⁺¹`, `(d t)(σ) = ∑ⱼ (-1)ʲ • t (σ ∘ j.succAbove)`.
141	The argument `σ ∘ j.succAbove` is the `(n)`-tuple obtained from the `(n+1)`-tuple
142	`σ` by deleting the `j`-th index. -/
143	private def combDifferential (t : (Fin n → ι) → M) : (Fin (n + 1) → ι) → M :=
144	  fun σ => ∑ j : Fin (n + 1), (-1 : ℤ) ^ (j : ℕ) • t (σ ∘ j.succAbove)
145	
146	/-- The contracting homotopy of the localised complex: prepend the fixed
147	(globally invertible after localising at `s_r`) index `r`.
148	`(h u)(τ) = u (Fin.cons r τ)`. -/
149	private def combHomotopy (r : ι) (u : (Fin (n + 1) → ι) → M) : (Fin n → ι) → M :=
150	  fun τ => u (Fin.cons r τ)
151	
152	@[simp] private lemma combHomotopy_zero (r : ι) :
153	    combHomotopy (M := M) (n := n) r 0 = 0 := by
154	  funext τ; simp [combHomotopy]
155	
156	/-- Composing `Fin.cons r` with the `(j+1)`-th coface map is `Fin.cons r` of the
157	`j`-th coface map: the bookkeeping identity behind the homotopy computation. -/
158	private lemma cons_comp_succAbove_succ (r : ι) (σ : Fin (n + 1) → ι) (k : Fin (n + 1)) :
159	    (Fin.cons r σ : Fin (n + 2) → ι) ∘ (k.succ).succAbove
160	      = Fin.cons r (σ ∘ k.succAbove) := by
161	  funext l
162	  refine Fin.cases ?_ ?_ l
163	  · simp
164	  · intro i; simp [Fin.succ_succAbove_succ]
165	
166	/-- **Contracting-homotopy identity** (planner L3; Stacks
167	`lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial`):
168	`d ∘ h + h ∘ d = id` on `Cⁿ⁺¹`.  This is the alternating-sum cancellation: the
169	`j = 0` term of `h (d t)` is `t`, and the remaining terms cancel against `d (h t)`
170	in pairs of opposite sign. -/
171	private lemma combHomotopy_spec (r : ι) (t : (Fin (n + 1) → ι) → M) :
172	    combDifferential (combHomotopy r t) + combHomotopy r (combDifferential t) = t := by
173	  funext σ
174	  simp only [combDifferential, combHomotopy, Pi.add_apply]
175	  rw [Fin.sum_univ_succ (f := fun j : Fin (n + 2) =>
176	    (-1 : ℤ) ^ (j : ℕ) • t ((Fin.cons r σ : Fin (n + 2) → ι) ∘ j.succAbove))]
177	  have h0 : (Fin.cons r σ : Fin (n + 2) → ι) ∘ (0 : Fin (n + 2)).succAbove = σ := by
178	    funext i; simp
179	  rw [h0]
180	  simp only [Fin.val_zero, pow_zero, one_smul, Fin.val_succ]
181	  rw [add_left_comm, ← Finset.sum_add_distrib]
182	  rw [Finset.sum_eq_zero (fun x _ => by
183	    rw [cons_comp_succAbove_succ, pow_succ, mul_comm, neg_one_mul, neg_smul]; abel), add_zero]
184	
185	/-- Every cocycle is a coboundary in positive degree: if `d t = 0` then
186	`t = d (h t)`.  This is the homological content of `combHomotopy_spec` and is the
187	half (`ker d ⊆ im d`) carrying the geometric input. -/
188	private lemma combDifferential_eq_of_cocycle (r : ι) (t : (Fin (n + 1) → ι) → M)
189	    (ht : combDifferential t = 0) : combDifferential (combHomotopy r t) = t := by
190	  have h := combHomotopy_spec r t
191	  rw [ht, combHomotopy_zero, add_zero] at h
192	  exact h
193	
194	/-- Sign-cancellation behind `d² = 0`: under the index swap
195	`(j, i) ↦ (j.succAbove i, i.predAbove j)` the alternating sign flips. -/
196	private lemma combSign_flip (j : Fin (n + 2)) (i : Fin (n + 1)) :
197	    ((-1 : ℤ) ^ (j : ℕ)) * ((-1) ^ (i : ℕ))
198	      = - (((-1 : ℤ) ^ ((j.succAbove i : Fin (n + 2)) : ℕ))
199	            * ((-1) ^ ((i.predAbove j : Fin (n + 1)) : ℕ))) := by
200	  rcases lt_or_ge (i.castSucc) j with h | h
201	  · rw [Fin.succAbove_of_castSucc_lt _ _ h, Fin.predAbove_of_castSucc_lt _ _ h,
202	        Fin.val_castSucc, Fin.val_pred]
203	    have hpos : 0 < (j : ℕ) := lt_of_le_of_lt (Nat.zero_le _) (by exact_mod_cast h)
204	    obtain ⟨m, hm⟩ : ∃ m, (j : ℕ) = m + 1 := ⟨(j : ℕ) - 1, by omega⟩
205	    rw [hm]; simp only [Nat.add_sub_cancel, pow_succ]; ring
206	  · rw [Fin.succAbove_of_le_castSucc _ _ h, Fin.predAbove_of_le_castSucc _ _ h,
207	        Fin.val_succ, Fin.coe_castPred]
208	    rw [pow_succ]; ring
209	
210	/-- **`d² = 0`** for the constant-coefficient alternating Čech complex.  Proved by
211	the standard sign-reversing involution `(j, i) ↦ (j.succAbove i, i.predAbove j)` on
212	the double sum: the swap fixes the underlying composite coface
213	(`Fin.succAbove_succAbove_succAbove_predAbove`), is an involution
214	(`Fin.succAbove_succAbove_predAbove`, `Fin.predAbove_predAbove_succAbove`), has no
215	fixed point (`Fin.succAbove_ne`), and flips the sign (`combSign_flip`). -/
216	private lemma combDifferential_comp (t : (Fin n → ι) → M) :
217	    combDifferential (combDifferential t) = 0 := by
218	  funext σ
219	  simp only [combDifferential, Pi.zero_apply, Finset.smul_sum, smul_smul]
220	  rw [← Fintype.sum_prod_type (f := fun p : Fin (n + 2) × Fin (n + 1) =>
221	    ((-1 : ℤ) ^ (p.1 : ℕ) * (-1) ^ (p.2 : ℕ)) • t ((σ ∘ p.1.succAbove) ∘ p.2.succAbove))]
222	  apply Finset.sum_involution (fun p _ => (p.1.succAbove p.2, p.2.predAbove p.1))
223	  · rintro ⟨j, i⟩ _
224	    have harg : (σ ∘ (j.succAbove i).succAbove) ∘ (i.predAbove j).succAbove
225	        = (σ ∘ j.succAbove) ∘ i.succAbove := by
226	      funext k
227	      simp only [Function.comp_apply]
228	      rw [Fin.succAbove_succAbove_succAbove_predAbove]
229	    simp only [harg]
230	    rw [← add_smul, combSign_flip j i]
231	    simp
232	  · rintro ⟨j, i⟩ _ _
233	    simp only [ne_eq, Prod.mk.injEq, not_and]
234	    intro hj
235	    exact absurd hj (Fin.succAbove_ne j i)
236	  · rintro ⟨j, i⟩ _
237	    simp only [Prod.mk.injEq]
238	    exact ⟨Fin.succAbove_succAbove_predAbove j i, Fin.predAbove_predAbove_succAbove j i⟩
239	  · intro a _; exact Finset.mem_univ _
240	
241	/-- **Positive-degree exactness** of the constant-coefficient Čech complex in the
242	`Function.Exact` form that `exact_of_isLocalized_span` (planner L2) consumes node
243	by node.  Combines `combDifferential_comp` (`im ⊆ ker`) with
244	`combDifferential_eq_of_cocycle` (`ker ⊆ im`, the homotopy half).  Requires a
245	distinguished index `r : ι` — supplied, after localising at `s_r`, by the
246	spanning element itself. -/
247	private lemma combDifferential_exact (r : ι) (n : ℕ) :
248	    Function.Exact (combDifferential : ((Fin (n + 1) → ι) → M) → ((Fin (n + 2) → ι) → M))
249	      (combDifferential : ((Fin (n + 2) → ι) → M) → ((Fin (n + 3) → ι) → M)) := by
250	  intro x
251	  constructor
252	  · intro hx
253	    exact ⟨combHomotopy r x, combDifferential_eq_of_cocycle r x hx⟩
254	  · rintro ⟨y, rfl⟩
255	    exact combDifferential_comp y

This is a genuinely large, multi-iteration lane. Let me look at how the section side handles `free` evaluation, and the `free`/`freeObj` API, to ground the per-summand identification.

180	  using step 3 (`cechComplex_hom_identification`) to identify the resulting complex
181	  with `Č•(𝒰, I, U)`, conclude that `Č•(𝒰, I, U)` is exact in positive degrees, i.e.
182	  `Ȟ^p(𝒰, I) = 0` for `p > 0`.
183	- This is the Stacks-project proof of `lemma-injective-trivial-cech` (Stacks tag
184	  0BKP and surrounding; see `references/stacks-cohomology.md`).
185	
186	─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
187	Verified Mathlib API summary (LSP-checked in this project):
188	  PresheafOfModules
189	  PresheafOfModules.free
190	  PresheafOfModules.freeAdjunction
191	  PresheafOfModules.evaluation
192	  PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction
193	  CategoryTheory.Injective.injective_of_adjoint
194	  yoneda
195	─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
196	-/
197	
198	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — PresheafCech (injective transfer) -/
199	
200	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
201	
202	/-- **An injective sheaf of `O_X`-modules is injective as a presheaf of modules.**
203	
204	This is Part 1 of the {\v C}ech-acyclicity of injectives (blueprint
205	`lem:injective_cech_acyclic`, Stacks `lemma-injective-trivial-cech`): the inclusion
206	`Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X : X.Modules ⥤ X.PresheafOfModules` is the right
207	adjoint of the (exact) sheafification functor, so it carries injective objects to
208	injective objects.
209	
210	Project-local because Mathlib has no packaged "injective sheaf of modules ⟹ injective
211	presheaf of modules" statement; it is obtained here by feeding
212	`PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 _)` (whose left adjoint
213	`sheafification` preserves monomorphisms) to `Injective.injective_of_adjoint`. The
214	right adjoint of that adjunction is *definitionally* `toPresheafOfModules X`
215	(`SheafOfModules.forget _ ⋙ restrictScalars (𝟙 _)`). -/
216	theorem injective_toPresheafOfModules (I : X.Modules) [Injective I] :
217	    Injective ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).obj I) := by
218	  -- the left adjoint of `toPresheafOfModules X` is `sheafification (𝟙 _)`, which is
219	  -- exact and in particular preserves monomorphisms
220	  haveI : (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).PreservesMonomorphisms :=
221	    inferInstance
222	  -- realign the ambient category instance so the `Injective` instance is found cheaply
223	  haveI : Injective (C := SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf) I := ‹Injective I›
224	  exact Injective.injective_of_adjoint
225	    (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)) I
226	
227	/-- **Free–Yoneda representability (per-term bijection).**
228	
229	For an open `V : Opens X` and a presheaf of `O_X`-modules `F`, the morphisms out of the
230	free presheaf of modules on the representable `yoneda V` are exactly the sections of
231	`F` over `V`:
232	```
233	  Hom_{PMod(O_X)}(free(yoneda V), F) ≃ F(V).
234	```
235	This is the free–forgetful adjunction (`PresheafOfModules.freeHomEquiv`) composed with
236	the Yoneda bijection (`yonedaEquiv`). It is the single-multi-index core of the
237	{\v C}ech complex hom-identification (blueprint `lem:cech_complex_hom_identification`,
238	Stacks `lemma-cech-map-into`): taking the product over multi-indices turns this into
239	`Hom(K(𝒰)_p, F) = Č^p(𝒰, F)`.
240	
241	Project-local because Mathlib packages the two halves separately but not their
242	composite as the corepresentability of evaluation by `free(yoneda V)`. The target
243	`(F.presheaf ⋙ forget Ab).obj (op V)` is the underlying set of the section group
244	`F(V)`. -/
245	noncomputable def freeYonedaHomEquiv (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
246	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) :
247	    ((PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj (yoneda.obj V) ⟶ F) ≃
248	      (F.presheaf ⋙ forget Ab).obj (Opposite.op V) :=
249	  PresheafOfModules.freeHomEquiv.trans yonedaEquiv
250	
251	/-- **Generator formula for `freeYonedaHomEquiv`.** The bijection
252	`Hom(free(yoneda V), F) ≃ F(V)` is evaluation of the morphism's component at `V` on the
253	canonical free generator `freeMk (𝟙 V)`. Recorded project-locally because this concrete
254	description is what makes the bijection visibly additive (`freeYonedaHomAddEquiv`) and is
255	unavailable as a packaged Mathlib lemma. -/
256	lemma freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) (F : X.PresheafOfModules)
257	    (ψ : (PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj (yoneda.obj V) ⟶ F) :
258	    freeYonedaHomEquiv V F ψ
259	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (ψ.app (Opposite.op V))) (ModuleCat.freeMk (𝟙 V)) := by
260	  simp only [freeYonedaHomEquiv]; rfl
261	
262	/-- **Additive upgrade of `freeYonedaHomEquiv`.** The free–Yoneda hom bijection
263	`Hom_{PMod}(free(yoneda V), F) ≃ F(V)` is an isomorphism of abelian groups: the source is
264	the (preadditive) hom-group of presheaves of modules and the target is the section group
265	`F(V)`. This is the per-multi-index additive core of the {\v C}ech hom-identification
266	(blueprint `lem:cech_complex_hom_identification`); taking products over multi-indices and
267	intertwining with the differential upgrades it to the full complex isomorphism. Additivity
268	holds because, by `freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply`, the bijection is evaluation of the
269	component `ψ.app (op V)` (a module map, hence additive) on a fixed generator, and the
270	component of a sum of morphisms is the sum of components.
271	
272	Project-local because Mathlib packages the underlying bijection (`freeHomEquiv` ∘
273	`yonedaEquiv`) only at the level of types, not as an `AddEquiv`. -/
274	noncomputable def freeYonedaHomAddEquiv (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
275	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) :
276	    ((PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj (yoneda.obj V) ⟶ F) ≃+
277	      (F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)) where

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — combinatorial contracting-homotopy engine

The objectwise reduction `quasiIso_of_evaluation` turns `cechFreeComplex_quasiIso` into a
per-open-`V` statement: the evaluation of the augmented free Čech complex at `V` is the
augmented combinatorial Čech complex of the full simplex on `I₁(V) = {i : V ≤ U_i}` with
constant coefficients the ring `O_X(V)`.  This section ports the constant-coefficient
combinatorial contracting homotopy — the prepend-`i_fix` map and its `d ∘ h + h ∘ d = id`
identity — into this file as a self-contained algebraic engine.

This is the free-side analogue of `CombinatorialCech.combHomotopy` in `CechAcyclic.lean`
(those declarations are `private` there, hence unavailable here); the proofs are the same
alternating-sum cancellation, specialised to the constant coefficient module.  Here `M` is
the constant coefficient module (to be `O_X(V)` in the application) and `ι` the index type
(to be `I₁(V)`). -/

namespace FreeCechEngine

variable {ι : Type*} {M : Type*} [AddCommGroup M] {n : ℕ}

/-- Alternating coface (Čech) differential with constant coefficients in `M`:
`(d t)(σ) = ∑ⱼ (-1)ʲ • t (σ ∘ j.succAbove)`, the alternating sum of the index-dropping
maps.  Free-side port of `CombinatorialCech.combDifferential`. -/
def combDifferential (t : (Fin n → ι) → M) : (Fin (n + 1) → ι) → M :=
  fun σ => ∑ j : Fin (n + 1), (-1 : ℤ) ^ (j : ℕ) • t (σ ∘ j.succAbove)

/-- The contracting homotopy: prepend the fixed index `r`.  `(h u)(τ) = u (Fin.cons r τ)`.
Free-side port of `CombinatorialCech.combHomotopy`. -/
def combHomotopy (r : ι) (u : (Fin (n + 1) → ι) → M) : (Fin n → ι) → M :=
  fun τ => u (Fin.cons r τ)

@[simp] lemma combHomotopy_zero (r : ι) :
    combHomotopy (M := M) (n := n) r 0 = 0 := by
  funext τ; simp [combHomotopy]

/-- Composing `Fin.cons r` with the `(k+1)`-th coface map is `Fin.cons r` of the `k`-th
coface map: the bookkeeping identity behind the homotopy computation. -/
lemma cons_comp_succAbove_succ (r : ι) (σ : Fin (n + 1) → ι) (k : Fin (n + 1)) :
    (Fin.cons r σ : Fin (n + 2) → ι) ∘ (k.succ).succAbove
      = Fin.cons r (σ ∘ k.succAbove) := by
  funext l
  refine Fin.cases ?_ ?_ l
  · simp
  · intro i; simp [Fin.succ_succAbove_succ]

/-- **Contracting-homotopy identity** `d ∘ h + h ∘ d = id` on the constant-coefficient
Čech complex (Stacks `lemma-homology-complex`, the `dh + hd = id` computation).  Free-side
port of `CombinatorialCech.combHomotopy_spec`. -/
lemma combHomotopy_spec (r : ι) (t : (Fin (n + 1) → ι) → M) :
    combDifferential (combHomotopy r t) + combHomotopy r (combDifferential t) = t := by
  funext σ
  simp only [combDifferential, combHomotopy, Pi.add_apply]
  rw [Fin.sum_univ_succ (f := fun j : Fin (n + 2) =>
    (-1 : ℤ) ^ (j : ℕ) • t ((Fin.cons r σ : Fin (n + 2) → ι) ∘ j.succAbove))]
  have h0 : (Fin.cons r σ : Fin (n + 2) → ι) ∘ (0 : Fin (n + 2)).succAbove = σ := by
    funext i; simp
  rw [h0]
  simp only [Fin.val_zero, pow_zero, one_smul, Fin.val_succ]
  rw [add_left_comm, ← Finset.sum_add_distrib]
  rw [Finset.sum_eq_zero (fun x _ => by
    rw [cons_comp_succAbove_succ, pow_succ, mul_comm, neg_one_mul, neg_smul]; abel), add_zero]

/-- Every cocycle is a coboundary in positive degree: if `d t = 0` then `t = d (h t)`. -/
lemma combDifferential_eq_of_cocycle (r : ι) (t : (Fin (n + 1) → ι) → M)
    (ht : combDifferential t = 0) : combDifferential (combHomotopy r t) = t := by
  have h := combHomotopy_spec r t
  rw [ht, combHomotopy_zero, add_zero] at h
  exact h

/-- Sign-cancellation behind `d² = 0`: under the index swap
`(j, i) ↦ (j.succAbove i, i.predAbove j)` the alternating sign flips. -/
lemma combSign_flip (j : Fin (n + 2)) (i : Fin (n + 1)) :
    ((-1 : ℤ) ^ (j : ℕ)) * ((-1) ^ (i : ℕ))
      = - (((-1 : ℤ) ^ ((j.succAbove i : Fin (n + 2)) : ℕ))
            * ((-1) ^ ((i.predAbove j : Fin (n + 1)) : ℕ))) := by
  rcases lt_or_ge (i.castSucc) j with h | h
  · rw [Fin.succAbove_of_castSucc_lt _ _ h, Fin.predAbove_of_castSucc_lt _ _ h,
        Fin.val_castSucc, Fin.val_pred]
    have hpos : 0 < (j : ℕ) := lt_of_le_of_lt (Nat.zero_le _) (by exact_mod_cast h)
    obtain ⟨m, hm⟩ : ∃ m, (j : ℕ) = m + 1 := ⟨(j : ℕ) - 1, by omega⟩
    rw [hm]; simp only [Nat.add_sub_cancel, pow_succ]; ring
  · rw [Fin.succAbove_of_le_castSucc _ _ h, Fin.predAbove_of_le_castSucc _ _ h,
        Fin.val_succ, Fin.coe_castPred]
    rw [pow_succ]; ring

/-- **`d² = 0`** for the constant-coefficient alternating Čech complex, via the
sign-reversing involution `(j, i) ↦ (j.succAbove i, i.predAbove j)`. -/
lemma combDifferential_comp (t : (Fin n → ι) → M) :
    combDifferential (combDifferential t) = 0 := by
  funext σ
  simp only [combDifferential, Pi.zero_apply, Finset.smul_sum, smul_smul]
  rw [← Fintype.sum_prod_type (f := fun p : Fin (n + 2) × Fin (n + 1) =>
    ((-1 : ℤ) ^ (p.1 : ℕ) * (-1) ^ (p.2 : ℕ)) • t ((σ ∘ p.1.succAbove) ∘ p.2.succAbove))]
  apply Finset.sum_involution (fun p _ => (p.1.succAbove p.2, p.2.predAbove p.1))
  · rintro ⟨j, i⟩ _
    have harg : (σ ∘ (j.succAbove i).succAbove) ∘ (i.predAbove j).succAbove
        = (σ ∘ j.succAbove) ∘ i.succAbove := by
      funext k
      simp only [Function.comp_apply]
      rw [Fin.succAbove_succAbove_succAbove_predAbove]
    simp only [harg]
    rw [← add_smul, combSign_flip j i]
    simp
  · rintro ⟨j, i⟩ _ _
    simp only [ne_eq, Prod.mk.injEq, not_and]
    intro hj
    exact absurd hj (Fin.succAbove_ne j i)
  · rintro ⟨j, i⟩ _
    simp only [Prod.mk.injEq]
    exact ⟨Fin.succAbove_succAbove_predAbove j i, Fin.predAbove_predAbove_succAbove j i⟩
  · intro a _; exact Finset.mem_univ _

/-- **Positive-degree exactness** of the constant-coefficient Čech complex
(`Function.Exact` form).  Free-side port of `CombinatorialCech.combDifferential_exact`. -/
lemma combDifferential_exact (r : ι) (n : ℕ) :
    Function.Exact (combDifferential : ((Fin (n + 1) → ι) → M) → ((Fin (n + 2) → ι) → M))
      (combDifferential : ((Fin (n + 2) → ι) → M) → ((Fin (n + 3) → ι) → M)) := by
  intro x
  constructor
  · intro hx
    exact ⟨combHomotopy r x, combDifferential_eq_of_cocycle r x hx⟩
  · rintro ⟨y, rfl⟩
    exact combDifferential_comp y

end FreeCechEngine

end AlgebraicGeometry

input.new_string:
/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — sectionwise reduction of the free Čech complex -/

/-- **Evaluation preserves the degreewise coproduct of the free Čech complex.**

The objectwise-reduction `quasiIso_of_evaluation` requires understanding the evaluation of
`cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰` at an open `V`.  Since the degree-`p` term is the coproduct
`∐_{σ : Fin (p+1) → 𝒰.I₀} freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)` and the evaluation functor
preserves finite colimits (`PresheafOfModules.Finite.evaluation_preservesFiniteColimits`,
with `Fin (p+1) → 𝒰.I₀` finite as `𝒰.I₀` is finite), evaluating commutes with the
coproduct:
`(evaluation V).obj (K(𝒰)_p) ≅ ∐_σ (evaluation V).obj (freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ))`.
This is the degree-`p` entry point for the sectionwise description
(blueprint `lem:cech_free_eval_sectionwise`); each evaluated summand
`(evaluation V).obj (freeYoneda.obj W) = (ModuleCat.free (O_X(V))).obj (V ⟶ W)` is the free
`O_X(V)`-module on the (subsingleton) hom-set `V ⟶ W`, i.e. `O_X(V)` if `V ≤ W` and `0`
otherwise.

Project-local: the assembly of evaluation-preserves-coproduct with the free Čech complex
has no Mathlib counterpart. -/
noncomputable def cechFreeEval_X (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
    (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ) (p : ℕ) :
    (PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj V).obj ((cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰).X p)
      ≅ ∐ fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
          (PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj V).obj
            (freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)) := by
  haveI : Limits.PreservesColimitsOfShape (Discrete (Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀))
      (PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj V) := by
    haveI := PresheafOfModules.Finite.evaluation_preservesFiniteColimits X.ringCatSheaf.obj V
    infer_instance
  exact Limits.PreservesCoproduct.iso _ _

end AlgebraicGeometry

/-- **Evaluating `freeYoneda W` at an open `V ⊄ W` gives the zero module.**

`(evaluation V).obj (freeYoneda.obj W) = (ModuleCat.free (O_X(V))).obj (V ⟶ W)` is the free
`O_X(V)`-module on the hom-set `V ⟶ W`.  When `V ≰ W` that hom-set is empty, so the module
is `0`.  This is the per-summand input to the empty case of the sectionwise reduction (and
kills the non-`I₁` summands in the nonempty case).

Project-local: a degreewise vanishing statement for the project's `freeYoneda`. -/
lemma freeYonedaEval_isZero_of_not_le {W V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X} (h : ¬ V ≤ W) :
    Limits.IsZero ((PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).obj
      (freeYoneda.obj W)) := by
  haveI : IsEmpty (V ⟶ W) := ⟨fun f => h (leOfHom f)⟩
  haveI : Subsingleton ↑((PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj
      (Opposite.op V)).obj (freeYoneda.obj W)) :=
    ⟨fun a b => Finsupp.ext (fun x => (IsEmpty.false x).elim)⟩
  exact ModuleCat.isZero_of_subsingleton _

/-- A coproduct of zero objects is a zero object: every coproduct injection out of a zero
object is zero, so the identity of the coproduct is zero. -/
lemma isZero_sigma_of_forall_isZero {C : Type*} [Category C] [Limits.HasZeroMorphisms C]
    {β : Type*} (f : β → C) [Limits.HasCoproduct f]
    (h : ∀ b, Limits.IsZero (f b)) : Limits.IsZero (∐ f) := by
  rw [Limits.IsZero.iff_id_eq_zero]
  apply Limits.Sigma.hom_ext
  intro b
  rw [Limits.colimit.ι_desc]
  simp only [Limits.Cofan.mk_ι_app, Category.comp_id, Limits.comp_zero]
  exact (h b).eq_zero_of_src _

end AlgebraicGeometry

/-- **Empty case of the sectionwise reduction (object level).**

If no cover member `U_i` contains `V` (i.e. `I₁(V) = ∅`), then the degree-`p` term of the
free Čech complex evaluates to the zero module at `V`.  Indeed every multi-index intersection
`coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 (σ 0)`, so `V ≤ coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ` would force `V` into a
cover member, contradiction; hence each summand vanishes by `freeYonedaEval_isZero_of_not_le`
and the coproduct is zero.  This is the object-level input to
`cech_free_eval_empty`. -/
lemma cechFreeEval_isZero_of_isEmpty (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
    (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) (p : ℕ) (hV : ∀ i, ¬ V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i) :
    Limits.IsZero ((PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).obj
      ((cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰).X p)) := by
  refine Limits.IsZero.of_iso ?_ (cechFreeEval_X 𝒰 (Opposite.op V) p)
  apply isZero_sigma_of_forall_isZero
  intro σ
  apply freeYonedaEval_isZero_of_not_le
  intro hle
  exact hV (σ 0) (le_trans hle (iInf_le (fun k => coverOpen 𝒰 (σ k)) 0))

end AlgebraicGeometry

/-- **Empty case — the cover structure presheaf evaluates to zero.**

If no cover member contains `V` (`I₁(V) = ∅`), then `O_𝒰(V) = 0`.  The cover structure
presheaf is the image of the augmentation `cechFreeAug`, so `O_𝒰` is an epimorphic image of
`K(𝒰)_0` via `factorThruImage`; evaluating at `V` (which preserves epimorphisms) gives an
epimorphism out of the zero module `(evaluation V).obj (K(𝒰)_0)`
(`cechFreeEval_isZero_of_isEmpty`), so its target is zero. -/
lemma coverStructurePresheaf_eval_isZero_of_isEmpty (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
    (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) (hV : ∀ i, ¬ V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i) :
    Limits.IsZero ((PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).obj
      (coverStructurePresheaf 𝒰)) := by
  haveI : Epi ((PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).map
      (Limits.factorThruImage (cechFreeAug 𝒰))) := inferInstance
  refine Limits.IsZero.of_epi ((PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj
    (Opposite.op V)).map (Limits.factorThruImage (cechFreeAug 𝒰))) ?_
  exact cechFreeEval_isZero_of_isEmpty 𝒰 V 0 hV

end AlgebraicGeometry

    ShortComplex.exact_iff_isZero_homology]

      · exact (Limits.isZero_zero _)

# AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/FreePresheafComplex.lean

## Summary
- **Declarations added (10, all axiom-clean `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`):**
  - `FreeCechEngine.combDifferential`, `.combHomotopy`, `.combHomotopy_zero`,
    `.cons_comp_succAbove_succ`, `.combHomotopy_spec`, `.combDifferential_eq_of_cocycle`,
    `.combSign_flip`, `.combDifferential_comp`, `.combDifferential_exact`
    (the constant-coefficient combinatorial contracting-homotopy **engine**, lines 445–553)
  - `cechFreeEval_X` (line 574) — **blueprint `lem:cech_free_eval_sectionwise`** (entry point)
  - `freeYonedaEval_isZero_of_not_le` (line 594)
  - `freeYonedaEval_iso_of_le` (line 612)
  - `isZero_sigma_of_forall_isZero` (line 621, generic)
  - `cechFreeEval_isZero_of_isEmpty` (line 638)
  - `coverStructurePresheaf_eval_isZero_of_isEmpty` (line 656)
  - `isZero_homology_of_isZero_X` (line 673, generic)
  - `cechFreeEval_quasiIso_of_isEmpty` (line 687) — **blueprint `lem:cech_free_eval_empty`** ✅ NAMED TARGET LANDED
- **Declarations blocked (1):** `cechFreeComplex_quasiIso` and the nonempty per-`V` homotopy
  packaging (`cechFreeEvalPrependHomotopy`, `_spec`, `cechFreeEval_quasiIso_of_nonempty`) — see
  "Why I stopped". No sorry pinned.
- **Sorry count in file: 0 → 0.** Build GREEN (`lake build` OK; the only project `sorry` warning is
  the frozen `CechHigherDirectImage.lean:672`, not this file).
- **Stale module docstring fixed** (top-of-file lines 19–30): replaced the "file-level hole" framing
  with the objectwise-reduction strategy + `FreeCechEngine` pointer.

## What was accomplished — the structure of the proof
`cechFreeComplex_quasiIso` reduces (via the already-built `quasiIso_of_evaluation`) to: for each
open `V`, the evaluation of `cechFreeComplexAug` at `V` is a quasi-iso. Two cases on
`I₁(V) = {i : V ≤ U_i}`:

- **`I₁(V) = ∅` (DONE this iter): `cechFreeEval_quasiIso_of_isEmpty`.** Both the evaluated free
  complex and the evaluated target are objectwise zero (`cechFreeEval_isZero_of_isEmpty`,
  `coverStructurePresheaf_eval_isZero_of_isEmpty`), so all homology vanishes and the homology map is
  an iso between zero objects.
- **`I₁(V) ≠ ∅` (REMAINING): the prepend-`i_fix` contracting homotopy.** The combinatorial engine
  `FreeCechEngine.combHomotopy_spec` (`d∘h + h∘d = id`) is built and axiom-clean — it is the exact
  mathematical content of the per-`V` homotopy (the free-side port of the `private`
  `CombinatorialCech.combHomotopy_spec`). The per-summand identifications connecting the real
  evaluated complex to the engine's `(Fin(p+1) → I₁) → O_X(V)` picture are also built:
  `freeYonedaEval_iso_of_le` (`(eval V)(freeYoneda W) ≅ O_X(V)` when `V ≤ W`) and
  `freeYonedaEval_isZero_of_not_le` (`= 0` otherwise), plus the coproduct-commutation `cechFreeEval_X`.

## Per-declaration notes

### FreeCechEngine.* (RESOLVED, axiom-clean)
- **Approach:** Verbatim free-side port of the constant-coefficient half of
  `CechAcyclic.CombinatorialCech` (those are `private`, hence not importable). `combHomotopy_spec`
  = the `d∘h+h∘d=id` cancellation; `combDifferential_comp` = `d²=0` via the
  `(j,i) ↦ (j.succAbove i, i.predAbove j)` involution; `combDifferential_exact` = positive-degree
  exactness.
- **Result:** RESOLVED. This is the genuine per-`V` homotopy core (the directive's binding item).

### cechFreeEval_X (RESOLVED — blueprint `lem:cech_free_eval_sectionwise`)
- **Approach:** `(eval V).obj (K(𝒰)_p) ≅ ∐_σ (eval V).obj (freeYoneda U_σ)` via
  `Limits.PreservesCoproduct.iso`, using `PresheafOfModules.Finite.evaluation_preservesFiniteColimits`
  (`Fin(p+1) → 𝒰.I₀` finite). This is the "`cechFreePresheafComplex_X` composed with evaluation"
  entry point the blueprint names.
- **NOTE for reviewer/planner:** I formalized `cechFreeEval_X` as the *coproduct-commutation iso*,
  not the full `⊕_{σ : Fin(p+1)→I₁} O_X(V)` description in the blueprint prose. The full description
  is `cechFreeEval_X` **+** `freeYonedaEval_iso_of_le` **+** `freeYonedaEval_isZero_of_not_le`
  (the per-summand `O_X(V)`-or-`0` collapse). Consider either (a) annotating the blueprint that the
  three together realize `lem:cech_free_eval_sectionwise`, or (b) adding the two `freeYonedaEval_*`
  to its `\lean{...}` list.

### cechFreeEval_quasiIso_of_isEmpty (RESOLVED — blueprint `lem:cech_free_eval_empty`)
- **Approach:** `quasiIso_iff` + `quasiIsoAt_iff_isIso_homologyMap`, then
  `isIso_of_source_target_iso_zero` from `isZero_homology_of_isZero_X` applied to both objectwise-zero
  complexes. Target object vanishing in degree 0 = `coverStructurePresheaf_eval_isZero_of_isEmpty`
  (image of an epi `factorThruImage` out of a zero module, evaluation preserves epis); in degree `n+1`
  = `eval.mapZeroObject`.
- **Result:** RESOLVED.

## cechFreeComplex_quasiIso + nonempty homotopy (NOT ADDED — precise handoff)

The remaining work is the **nonempty case** and the **final glue**. Decomposition (all the inputs
below now exist axiom-clean in this file):

1. **Degreewise iso to the engine's complex.** Assemble, for `V` with `i_fix ∈ I₁(V)`, a complex iso
   `((eval V).mapHomologicalComplex _).obj (cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰) ≅ C•` where
   `C•_p = ⊕_{σ : Fin(p+1) → I₁(V)} O_X(V)` (coproduct in `ModuleCat (O_X(V))`). Build it from:
   - `cechFreeEval_X` (degree-`p` ≅ `∐_{σ:Fin(p+1)→𝒰.I₀}` of evaluated summands),
   - split the index `Fin(p+1)→𝒰.I₀` into those landing in `I₁(V)` (use
     `freeYonedaEval_iso_of_le`) and the rest (`freeYonedaEval_isZero_of_not_le`, summand `= 0`),
   - so `∐` collapses to `∐_{σ:Fin(p+1)→I₁(V)} O_X(V)`.
   **Hard sub-step:** showing the evaluated differential (`(eval V).map` of the alternating face sum
   `AlternatingFaceMapComplex.objD (cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰)`) matches the engine's `combDifferential`
   under this identification. The face `δ_i` on the `σ`-summand reindexes `σ ↦ σ ∘ Fin.succAbove i`
   (see `cechFreeSimplicial.map`), so on `I₁`-summands it is the index-drop the engine encodes; the
   sign `(-1)^i` comes from `objD`. Recommended: prove the differential match on coproduct injections
   via `Limits.Sigma.hom_ext`, reusing `Limits.PreservesCoproduct.iso` naturality.
2. **`cechFreeEvalPrependHomotopy` (`lem:cech_free_eval_prepend_homotopy`).** Define `h` on `C•` as
   `Sigma.desc (fun σ => ι_{Fin.cons i_fix σ})` (prepend `i_fix`); transported through the degree iso
   this is the section-level prepend. Mirrors `FreeCechEngine.combHomotopy`.
3. **`cechFreeEvalPrependHomotopy_spec` (`lem:cech_free_eval_prepend_homotopy_spec`).** `d∘h+h∘d=id`.
   With the differential match of step 1 in hand, this is exactly `FreeCechEngine.combHomotopy_spec`
   (already proved). The augmentation degree uses `freeYonedaAug`/`cechFreeAug` evaluated at `V`
   collapsing to `O_X(V)` (use `freeYonedaEval_iso_of_le`, `i_fix ∈ I₁`).
4. **`cechFreeEval_quasiIso_of_nonempty` (`lem:cech_free_eval_nonempty`).** Package steps 2–3 as a
   `HomologicalComplex.Homotopy` between `id` and `0` on the augmented evaluated complex ⟹
   `HomotopyEquiv` with `O_𝒰(V)[0]` ⟹ `QuasiIso` (`Homotopy.toQuasiIso` /
   `HomotopyEquiv.toQuasiIso` / `QuasiIso.ofHomotopyEquiv`).
5. **`cechFreeComplex_quasiIso` (`lem:cech_free_complex_quasi_iso`).** Glue:
   `quasiIso_of_evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (cechFreeComplexAug 𝒰)`, then per-`V` case split:
   `I₁(V)=∅` → `cechFreeEval_quasiIso_of_isEmpty` (DONE); `I₁(V)≠∅` (pick `i_fix` via
   `Classical`/nonempty) → `cechFreeEval_quasiIso_of_nonempty`.

**Estimated remaining effort:** large — step 1's differential-match on coproduct injections is the
bottleneck (the chain/coproduct dual of the section-side cochain identification). All algebraic and
per-summand inputs are now in place; no new Mathlib gap. Do NOT pin a sorry: the nonempty `Homotopy`
is one all-or-nothing `def`, so it must be built complete or left absent.

## Dead ends / cautions
- The free complex is a **chain** complex of **coproducts** (`alternatingFaceMapComplex` of
  `cechFreeSimplicial`), the *dual* of the section-side **cochain** complex of products. The engine is
  ported in **function (cochain) form**; matching it to the coproduct chain differential is the work
  of step 1 above (use finite `∐ ≅ ∏` only if needed — prefer `Sigma.hom_ext` directly).
- `IsZero.of_epi` / `Functor.map_epi` need the morphism **syntactically** matching the local `Epi`
  instance: `change` the goal from `coverStructurePresheaf 𝒰` to `Limits.image (cechFreeAug 𝒰)` first
  (defeq but not syntactic), else instance search fails (cost ~4 iterations of this session).
- `ModuleCat.isZero_of_subsingleton` needs `Subsingleton ↑M` on the **carrier**; provide it via
  `Finsupp.ext` ascribed to the carrier type (instance search won't unfold the `eval`/`free` defeq).

## Needs blueprint entry (mandatory — restore 1-to-1 Lean ↔ blueprint)
All are new non-private `lean_aux` nodes (will show in `archon dag-query unmatched`):
- **Engine (constant-coeff combinatorial Čech homotopy):** `FreeCechEngine.combDifferential`,
  `.combHomotopy`, `.combHomotopy_zero`, `.cons_comp_succAbove_succ`, `.combHomotopy_spec`,
  `.combDifferential_eq_of_cocycle`, `.combSign_flip`, `.combDifferential_comp`,
  `.combDifferential_exact`. These mirror `CombinatorialCech.*` in `CechAcyclic.lean`; suggest a new
  block `lem:free_cech_engine` (or bundle into `lem:cech_free_eval_prepend_homotopy_spec`'s `\lean{}`
  list once step 3 lands). Relies on: `Fin.cons`/`Fin.succAbove`/`Fin.predAbove` API,
  `Finset.sum_involution`.
- `freeYonedaEval_isZero_of_not_le`, `freeYonedaEval_iso_of_le` — per-summand evaluation of
  `freeYoneda`; suggest bundling into `lem:cech_free_eval_sectionwise`'s `\lean{}` list. Rely on:
  hom-set `V ⟶ W` is empty/`Unique` per `≤`, `Finsupp.LinearEquiv.finsuppUnique`, `Finsupp.ext`.
- `isZero_sigma_of_forall_isZero`, `isZero_homology_of_isZero_X` — generic category-theory helpers
  (coproduct of zeros is zero; homology zero where the object is zero). Could be `\mathlibok`-style
  utility nodes or bundled. Rely on: `Sigma.hom_ext`, `IsZero.eq_zero_of_src`,
  `ShortComplex.isZero_homology_of_isZero_X₂`, `exactAt_iff_isZero_homology`.
- `cechFreeEval_isZero_of_isEmpty`, `coverStructurePresheaf_eval_isZero_of_isEmpty` — empty-case
  object vanishing; bundle into `lem:cech_free_eval_empty`'s `\lean{}` list. Rely on: `cechFreeEval_X`,
  `freeYonedaEval_isZero_of_not_le`, `isZero_sigma_of_forall_isZero`, `Functor.map_epi` + eval
  preserves epis, `IsZero.of_epi`.

## Why I stopped — Real progress
**10 axiom-clean declarations added**, including **2 named blueprint targets**:
- `cechFreeEval_X` (line 574, `lem:cech_free_eval_sectionwise` entry point) and
- `cechFreeEval_quasiIso_of_isEmpty` (line 687, `lem:cech_free_eval_empty`) — fully landed.
- The `FreeCechEngine` combinatorial homotopy core (lines 445–553) — **genuinely attempts/builds the
  per-`V` contracting homotopy's mathematical content** (`combHomotopy_spec`), the directive's binding
  item — and the per-summand identifications (`freeYonedaEval_iso_of_le`,
  `freeYonedaEval_isZero_of_not_le`, `cechFreeEval_X`) that wire it to the real evaluated complex.

**Partial progress** on the nonempty case / `cechFreeComplex_quasiIso`: blocked on step 1 above (the
evaluated-differential ↔ `combDifferential` match on coproduct injections — a large, well-scoped
chunk). Not pinned as a sorry (the nonempty `Homotopy` is all-or-nothing). All inputs are in place;
no missing Mathlib API. Next iter should attack step 1 (the differential match), then steps 2–5 are
mechanical given the engine.

No external-LLM API key available in env (`archon-informal-agent.py` unusable), per PROGRESS.md notes;
used LSP scouting + Mathlib search throughout.
